### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Clemens-Brentano-Grundschule positions itself as a **musikalische Grundschule im Kiez** (musical primary school in the neighborhood), emphasizing a harmonious blend of learning, relationships, and life as a shared space for teachers, educators, students, and parents.[1][4][9] Core values include fostering **tolerance, respect, appreciation, and democratic understanding** through projects on non-violent conflict resolution, class councils from grade 3, conflict mediators, student parliament, and mediation based on the Bensberger model.[4] It prioritizes musical education with regular performances like Hausmusikabend and theater, alongside media literacy, gifted education, reading promotion, and preparation for the digital age via a 2019 media concept.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after poet Clemens Brentano (1778-1842) since 1968, the school occupies a **listed monumental complex** in pavilion style, built starting 1898 with main building and decentralized wings around a central courtyard, featuring natural surroundings, light, air, and movement.[1][3] Facilities include a tree-lined schoolyard with student-maintained beds and sculptures, playgrounds, school garden with pond and beehive, rabbit hutch, NaWi room, PC room with smartboards, school library, and 20 computers with internet.[1][5] Community involvement is strong via the **Förderverein** (parent-teacher association) supporting events like summer festivals and Christmas markets, student-led material provision during breaks, and initiatives like Schulzonen-Aktionstag for safer streets.[1][7][8] Leadership includes principal Angela Sommerlatte (since 2015/16) and deputy Johannes Girke (since 2025/26).[7] It serves ~450-470 students in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf, Lichterfelde-West.[3][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public **Grundschule** (grades 1-6), the curriculum covers Deutsch, Fremdsprachen (English or French from grade 3), Mathematik, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Naturwissenschaften, Sachunterricht, Lebenskunde, religions, Kunst, Musik, and Sport.[1][3][4] Special programs include **LemaS** (Leistung macht Schule) for gifted students since 2018 as a Berlin pilot and multiplier school, Englischprojekt Interact (theater in grade 6), Leseförderung, and Begabungskurse like Matheprofis.[2][8] Grades 5-6 offer elective courses such as student newspaper, research/experiments, clay work, or revue.[4] Media competency workshops and open all-day care (OGB) with year-group learning groups are available.[2][6] Musical focus includes Hausmusikabend and music school after-school courses.[1][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse **extracurricular activities (AGs)** like Basketball, Chor, Fußball, Leichtathletik, Judo, Kinderyoga, Zirkus, Leseratten, Leseprofis, Tanz, Philosophieren, Debattieren, Experimentelles Gestalten, Schauspiel, and Matheprofis, plus offerings from Goethe-Gymnasium and Leo-Borchard-Musikschule.[1][3][5][6] Experiences include warm school lunches (Mensa), after-school care (Nachmittagsbetreuung), excursions to theaters/museums/sports events, class trips, project days every two years, student parliament, conflict mediation, and hands-on projects like Barfußpfad or peace symbols.[5][8] Sport is emphasized with district/national competition successes; November is dedicated to reading.[1][5][8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Notable successes include excellent results at the 2025 Bolyai Mathematics Team Competition with 41 participants from grades 3-6 competing regionally/nationally, a 2012 Schulhomepage-Award, and strong sports performances.[8] As a LemaS pilot (2018-2023) and multiplier school (to 2027), it advances gifted education strategies.[2] Community highlights: student budget management (€1,500), Schulzonen demonstration with 470 children, and peace formation on the yard.[8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Information on **Schulanmeldung** (school enrollment) is available via dedicated page, including Schulprogramm and Schulvertrag; as a public school in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (25th Grundschule), enrollment follows local district procedures for grades 1-6.[1][3]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Comenius-Schule is an inclusive special needs school emphasizing autism and learning support, aiming for maximum participation of all children in inclusive classrooms where two children with autism are integrated per class.[1][9] Its philosophy promotes mutual learning, recognizing individual learning paces and heterogeneity as enriching, with principles of openness, respect, tolerance, and self-organized learning to foster self-, social-, and subject-specific competencies.[1][5] Teachers guide development fairly, supporting joy in learning while serving as role models.[5][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located at Gieselerstr. 4, 10713 Berlin (Wilmersdorf, Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district), this public school serves Grundschule (primary), Sekundarstufe I, and II levels with focuses on autism and learning disabilities.[2][3][6] It includes a branch at Spandauer Damm 130, 14050 Berlin, and hospital classes (Klinikklassen) at Klinikum Westend for children in psychiatric care averaging six weeks.[2][6] Facilities feature inclusive classrooms, at least two computers per room, media workshop, and café; community involves close collaboration with internal/external partners, parents, and offene Ganztagbetreuung (open all-day care).[1][2] Leadership: Ulrike Walther; contact: +49 30 8649950, Schulleitung@comenius.schule.berlin.de.[2][6]

### 3. CURRICURICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Classes are age-homogeneous with max 23 students, staffed by two teachers plus a support person, following Berlin's framework curriculum with inclusive design emphasizing errors as learning aids.[1] Autism support includes small classes (up to 6 students) with team teaching (teacher + pedagogical assistant + optional educator), individualized plans focusing on self-responsibility, techniques, self-esteem, and frustration tolerance.[7] Programs cover Grundschule integration, IBA (integrated vocational preparation), productive learning, career orientation, hospital external branches, and small classes for autism/Asperger; languages include English; possible qualification: Berufsbildungsreife.[1][2][9] Additional: sensory learning, practical skills, leveled groups in German/math.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience high individual support in heterogeneous, inclusive settings promoting participation, self-reliance, and social skills via Pausengespräche, mediation, Fair Player (anti-bullying), social learning, prevention, and girls' groups.[1][8] Extracurriculars (AGs) include conflict training, bookbinding, cooking, cultural trips, newspaper, textiles, dance, theater, sports, magic, choir, musical (with Villa Comenius), chess, painting, basketball.[2] Small autism classes emphasize personalized development, realistic self-image, and handling assessments; all-day care and partners enhance experiences.[2][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific achievements or outcomes like test scores or graduation rates are detailed in available content; focus is on qualitative goals like unfolding potentials, community strength, and sustainable development (noted in related profiles).[4][9] The school highlights long-term expertise in autism support as a quality standard.[7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free school, admissions target students with special needs in autism and learning, including integrated primary classes and small autism/Asperger groups; hospital classes serve short-term psychiatric inpatients.[1][2][7] No specific process detailed; contact school leadership for inquiries.[2]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Conrad-Schule emphasizes innovative, nature-based, and inclusive education, participating in the "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage (SOR-SMC)" project to promote anti-racism and courage.[1] It integrates **Montessori-Pädagogik** principles, such as Freiarbeit (free work) outdoors, social learning, and year-group-spanning inclusive learning for grades 1-3 and 4-6, with a strong focus on outdoor experiences like Waldklassen (forest classes).[2][4][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after Carl Heinrich Wilhelm Conrad (1822-1899), founder of the "Villenkolonie Alsen" (later Wannsee community), the school originated from the former Gemeindeschule Wannsee.[1][3] Construction began with a building in 1897-98 at Charlottenstraße, followed by a larger school in 1903-04 at Schulstraße and a director's residence; a 1982-84 half-circular Grundschulneubau by Horst Hielscher and Georg P. Mügge harmoniously connects to the Altbau.[3] Facilities include a historic Altbau (massive construction from the late 19th century), three buildings near former Wannsee Rathaus, two identical Sporthallen with Prallschutz (impact protection) and renovated changing rooms, a **Schulgarten** for studying fauna and flora, extensive shaded outdoor play areas with large equipment, and a dedicated Ganztagsbetreuung building.[1][4] The community supports initiatives like Waldklassen since 2016/2017 and serves around 460 students, with strong parental involvement.[1][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Ganztagsschule** (all-day school), the curriculum follows standard Berlin regulations, allowing seamless transfer to other schools, with core subjects like reading, writing, and math taught mornings indoors (8-10/8:30 AM), followed by 3.5+ hours daily outdoors from ~10 AM to 1:30 PM lunch.[2][4][5] **Waldklassen** (forest classes, starting 2016 for grades 1-6) teach language, mathematics, sports (Lernen in Bewegung), dance/music, crafts/art, and science/nature studies in the forest, school garden, or yard, using tools like Bollerwagen with magnifiers, knives, tablets, and books; other classes use indoor spaces or the "Draußen-Klassenzimmer."[2][4][5] After-school includes AGs (working groups) like literature club, sports, theater, and English.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience hands-on, nature-immersed learning, such as building stock circles with clay, estimating pinecones in Hula Hoop circles, investigating geological features like Lehmhügel (clay hills), and daily outdoor sessions with Frühstückspausen (breakfast breaks), Ruhezeit (quiet time), and return-to spots for experiments.[2][4][5] Inclusive mixing of children with and without special needs fosters attention and learning motivation; ~350 of 460 students opt for Ganztag with AGs and free play until 6 PM, supported by 22 Erzieher, integration specialists, and social work (Schulstation Dschungel, new carrier since July 2024).[4] Facilities enable play, exploration, and social activities under trees.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school pioneered Waldklassen in Berlin for inclusive learning, inspired by Waldkitas, with no legal barriers to outdoor teaching in state schools; it reports heightened student attention and learning readiness across all children.[2][5] Ongoing renovations (e.g., 475,000€ roof work by 2021, Sporthallen upgrades) reflect community and district investment.[1] Participation in SOR-SMC highlights social achievements.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
No specific admissions details are available from the provided content; as a public Grundschule, enrollment follows standard Berlin public school procedures based on residence.[1]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Demokratische Schule X operates as a democratic school emphasizing **self-determination**, where students decide what, when, and how they learn, with staff acting as learning companions rather than traditional teachers. Core principles include **democracy** through the Schulversammlung (school assembly), where students and staff jointly decide on community matters like rules, budget, and hiring via majority vote with equal voices; and **equality**, with uniform rules for all and a Rechtsversammlung (judicial assembly) handling violations. The approach rejects grades, mandatory tests, or exams, focusing on self-directed learning in mixed-age groups from grades 1-10 to foster inclusion, individual development, and real-world preparation without early tracking or retention.[1][2][4][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded on December 1, 2010, the school is a private Gemeinschaftsschule (community school) owned by Demokratische Bildung Berlin e.V., serving approximately 95 students in 2023/24. Located at Ruppiner Chaussee 209-211, 13503 Berlin (Heiligensee), it receives partial public funding (about two-thirds of state schools) but funds building expansions privately. Facilities improvements are supported by funders like Software AG Stiftung (remise renovation), Kommunalinvestitionsförderungsprogramm, Bundesförderung für effiziente Gebäude, Ganztagsbetreuung infrastructure program, and DigitalPakt Schule. Opening hours: Mon/Wed/Thu 7:30-17:00, Tue/Fri 7:30-16:00, with after-school care (Hort) until 18:00 if needed.[1][3][4][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school covers **grades 1-10** (Grundschule through Sekundarstufe) in mixed-age, heterogeneous groups without strict grade separation, promoting inclusion for students with and without special needs. Self-directed learning prevails, with no grades, retention, or mandatory internal assessments; qualifications like BBR, eBBR, or MSA are obtained via external exams. Languages include English (1st foreign language) and French (2nd). It offers offene Ganztagbetreuung (open all-day care), schulanfangsphase with cross-grade groups, and digital infrastructure via DigitalPakt.[1][2][4][6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students shape their daily school life through self-determination in mixed-age settings, collaborating on shared interests rather than age-based classes. Democratic structures like twice-weekly Schulversammlung enable participation in decisions on rules, spending, and activities. Extracurriculars include all-day care (Hort), free lunches for Grundschule students, and community events like open doors (e.g., 01.10.2025) and info evenings (e.g., for girls on 25.02.2026). No specific sports, arts, or exchanges are detailed, but the model supports peer teaching and inclusive projects.[1][4][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or student outcome data (e.g., exam results) are mentioned. The school's concept was approved in July 2010 by Berlin school authorities, confirming it meets public school standards and recognizes special pedagogical interest under Article 7(5) of the Basic Law. It participates in the Gemeinschaftsschule pilot.[2][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions start with an online or downloadable Interessenbekundungsbogen (expression of interest form); the Aufnahmekomitee reviews and offers a school tour, interview, and trial week if space allows. Priority considers balanced age/gender distribution via an "Aufnahmeampel" (admissions light system). Response may take up to 2 months.[Admissions Page]

**Fees** (income-scaled per TKBG levels, effective 01.08.2023; excludes separate Hort contribution and meals):
- **Grundschulalter (grades 1-6)**: 45-352 €/month, varying by income (e.g., <1,300 €/month brutto: 45-89 €; up to 8,9700 €/year: 352 € for 1 child), number of children in household (lower with more), and siblings at DSX (e.g., 3+ siblings: as low as 45 €).[Fee Page][PDF Grundschüler]
- **Ab 7. Jahrgangsstufe (secondary)**: 75-598 €/month (tables in PDF).[Fee Page][PDF Sekundarschüler]
- **Aufnahmegebühr (enrollment fee)**: 1000 € standard; 500 € reduced (for Wohngeld, ALG-II, BAföG recipients or equivalent income; applies to 2nd+ siblings regardless). Payable in 3 monthly installments (+5 € fee); up to 6/9 for 2/3 siblings; more by agreement (+1 €/extra rate).[Fee Page]
- **Essensgeld (meals)**: Free for Grundschule; Sekundarstufe 37 €/month (20 € with Berlinpass).[Fee Page]
- Fees are 30% tax-deductible up to 5,000 €/year as special expenses. School receives ~2/3 state funding, necessitating fees for building costs.[Fee Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Deutsch Skandinavische Gemeinschaftsschule (DSG) blends Scandinavian language and culture with German education through project-based learning (PBL), emphasizing individual development, lifelong learning, multilingualism, purposeful digitalization, and community.[1][2] Core values include viewing learning as an adventure without "mistakes" but with challenges, promoting multilingualism in Danish, Swedish, Norwegian, German, and more, using digital tools to empower active learners, and fostering community through daily shared cooking and three healthy meals.[2][4] It draws from Scandinavian pedagogy like "Skola 2000," with flexible "Lernlandschaften" (learning landscapes), individual development plans, and teachers as learning companions.[3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2012 by six parents, starting with 27 students, DSG has grown to about 200 students and is a state-recognized substitute school (Ersatzschule) under the Montessori Stiftung Berlin.[1][2][4] Under the patronage of HRH Princess Benedikte of Denmark since 2015, it operates as a public school in private sponsorship (freie Trägerschaft).[2][4][5] Located at Machonstr. 54, 12105 Berlin (Tempelhof-Schöneberg/Mariendorf), it features a full-day concept with structured rituals, learning, breaks, and meals; two learning companions per class support cross-grade, project-based, and extracurricular learning.[1][2][5] Current projects include climate-friendly outdoor redesign (2025, funded by BENE 2 and EU regional funds).[2] An international team from over ten nations serves a diverse community.[4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
DSG serves grades 1-10 as a Gemeinschaftsschule, leading to the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA), with gymnasiale Oberstufe options via Montessori Stiftung partners.[1][2][5] Bilingual instruction (German-Danish, German-Swedish, German-Norwegian) up to grade 6, shifting to Scandinavian language courses from grade 7; English as first foreign language, Spanish as second.[2][5] Follows Berlin's framework curriculum with Scandinavian influences, year-crossing groups in early phases, project- and product-oriented learning, and open ganztag (full-day) with obligatory Hort (after-school care) for grades 1-6 focusing on languages and activities.[1][2][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Daily structure includes three healthy communal meals (self-cooked), project-based and outdoor learning, AGs (extracurricular clubs), and digital tools for active engagement.[2][7] Full-day program with fixed rituals, breaks, and learning times; obligatory Hort for grades 1-6 emphasizes language play, shared activities, and concept continuation.[2][7] Flexible learning landscapes mix ages and languages; community focus with everyone contributing.[3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-recognized Ersatzschule status; patronage by Princess Benedikte since 2015.[2][4][5] No specific awards, rankings, or exam results detailed in content, though Berlin official listings confirm approvals for bilingual programs, ganztag, and open ganztag.[5][6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admission prioritizes active Scandinavian language speakers (60% of places), with 40% for German-speaking children eager to learn one; siblings and Kita Lillebror children get priority if criteria met; accepts transfers up to grade 10.[2] Process: Check criteria, submit non-binding form, attend open house (next: Oct 9, in-person or digital), hospitation/kennenlerntag, family interview, then decision.[2] **Fees are income-dependent (einkommensabhängig)**: Primary (grades 1-6) at 3.2% of guardians' income; secondary (7-10) at 4.2%, collected monthly by direct debit; sibling discounts (100% first child, 60% second, 40% further); exemptions/hardship cases available; Hort obligatory for 1-6 (income-calculated via Jugendamt, lower tuition rate applies).[7] Additional: **one-time enrollment fee >500 EUR** (installments for low-income); **annual 150 EUR** materials (school start); **monthly 65 EUR** school meals. Bildungs- und Teilhabepaket subsidies for low-income families cover meals, materials, trips. Detailed rules in Schulgeld- und Schulkostenregelung (Aug 2022).[7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The **dreieins-Grundschule Berlin Kaulsdorf** operates under the core pillars of **Kreativität (creativity), Internationalität (internationality), and Nachhaltigkeit (sustainability)**, believing every child has unique talents to discover and nurture. It follows the Berlin state curriculum while enriching it with creative pedagogical methods, early foreign language acquisition, and projects promoting environmental awareness. The approach emphasizes meeting children where they are, fostering independence, self-confidence, sensory stimulation, and proactive involvement through age-specific activities aligned with the Berlin Educational Program and dreieins' creativity pedagogy.[1][2][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Operated by **dreieins Innovative Pädagogik gGmbH** (headquartered at Storkower Straße 99, 10407 Berlin), the school lacks specific founding details in the content but holds ISO 9001:2015 certification for its management system (valid until 2025-12-17, first certified 2013) covering grundschulpädagogische Bildung and supplementary care at this location. Located at **Peter-Huchel-Str. 35, 12619 Berlin (Hellersdorf district)**, it shares a campus with affiliated Kitas (Kindertagesstätten), including a bilingual D/E version, featuring spacious rooms and a large play garden. Community ties include close parent collaboration, events like Fasching and Fußballturnier, and partnerships with Musikschule Hugo Distler (musical early education) and Spielsprachschule Berlin Abrakadabra.[3][4][5][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **private Grundschule (primary school, grades 1-6)** and state-recognized Ersatzschule, it implements the Berlin Rahmenlehrplan comprehensively, offering **offene Ganztagsbetreuung (OGB)** and Schulanfangsphase with year-group learning clusters. Languages include **English from grade 1**, introductions to **Arabic or Chinese** from grade 1, and **French from grade 2**. Creativity areas supplement core subjects; pre-school activities introduce coding (Bee-Bot), body awareness/math, and robotics. No details on upper secondary pathways like Abitur or MSA.[1][5][6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in creativity-focused projects, foreign language immersion, sustainability initiatives, music via external partners, and play-based learning with daily singing and movement. Extracurriculars include sports (e.g., Fußballturnier for grades 1-6), Fasching celebrations, programming/robotics in pre-school, bilingual projects on diversity/body awareness, and Lantern festivals uniting school and Kitas. Ganztagsbetreuung supports extended hours; campus events foster community.[1][3][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified under **DIN EN ISO 9001:2015** by TÜV NORD CERT for quality management in primary education and care (scope includes this Kaulsdorf site; valid 2022-12-18 to 2025-12-17). No rankings, test scores, or graduation outcomes mentioned.[PDF: http://www.dreieins.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/137367-dreieins-innovattive-paedagogik-ggmbh-qm-wa-22-rs-de.pdf]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve open enrollment with year-round Kita starts possible; limited spots available for Oct. 2024-Sept. 2025 and from Oct. 2025. Events include Campus Day (20 Feb 2026, 15:00+), Tag der offenen Tür, and Infoveranstaltungen; contact Schulbüro (Mo-Thu 8:00-15:30, Fri 8:00-14:30) or email grundschule-kaulsdorf@dreieins.org. School leadership: Herr El Cherif (Leitung), Frau Abouelela (stellv.). **No tuition or fee amounts in EUR** (e.g., Schulgeld, Beitrag, Gebühr) found across main pages, fee pages, or PDFs; all fee-related flags are False. Inquire directly via Tel. 030 92901840 or Hort 030 92901830.[3][5][6]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The **dreieins-Grundschule Berlin-Pankow** operates under the dreieins Innovative Pädagogik gGmbH, emphasizing that **every child is gifted** and should explore the world through all senses. Core values are **Kreativität (creativity), Internationalität (internationality), and Nachhaltigkeit (sustainability)**. The approach integrates creativity pedagogy with performance-oriented education, including specialized creativity subjects like "Darstellendes Spiel" (performing arts), "Tanz/Bewegung" (dance/movement), "Entdecken-Erforschen-Erfinden" (discover-research-invent), strategic games/chess, and digital media, alongside the Berlin Bildungsprogramm.[Main Page][About Page][Fee/Tuition Pages]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as part of dreieins Innovative Pädagogik gGmbH (20+ years of operation), the school holds **ISO 9001:2015 certification** for its management system in grundschulpädagogische Bildung and supplementary care, valid until 2025-12-17, covering the Pankow site among others. Located at **Bizetstraße 64, 13088 Berlin** (Pankow district), it offers **betreuung from 6:00 to 18:00** with small classes (17-24 children). The campus is described as green and beautiful in related sites (e.g., Kaulsdorf with ~200 children), fostering a learning organization with family collaboration and diverse staff qualifications.[Main Page][Fee/Tuition Pages][PDF: ISO Cert]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Grundschule (primary school, grades 1-6)**, it provides **leistungsorientierter Teilungsunterricht** (performance-oriented differentiated instruction) in Deutsch, Mathematik, languages, and creativity subjects. Languages include **English and Arabisch/Chinesisch from grade 1, Französisch from grade 2**. Sample schedules (Musterstundenpläne) are available for grades 1-5. It aligns with Berlin standards while emphasizing creativity and follows into partnerships with affiliated Kitas.[Main Page][Fee/Tuition Pages][PDF Documents]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Daily structure supports holistic development with **healthy meals** (breakfast, lunch, snacks), **outdoor play areas**, singing, movement, and **English language offers** by native speakers. Extracurriculars include projects (e.g., garden-based candle-making), theater pedagogy, music school cooperation (Hugo Distler), and events like Fasching (carnival), SCHILF-Tag (training day), and open doors. After-school hort/betreuung includes supervision, projects, and excursions.[Main Page][About Page][PDF: Tagesabläufe]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Students achieved **nationwide 1st place in 4th grade** at the Heureka! Wettbewerb "Mensch und Natur" (human and nature). The organization maintains ISO 9001:2015 certification since 2013 (renewed 2022).[Main Page][PDF: ISO Cert]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve online forms via dreieins.org, with personal introductions, no prior registration needed for open days (e.g., 01.02.2025). Contact: kundenmanagement@dreieins.org or 030 420 246 970. **No specific fee/tuition amounts in EUR** are mentioned on the Pankow pages or PDFs; one fee-related page (job posting for Kaulsdorf) discusses staff benefits like "personalvergünstigung" (staff discounts) for school places but lists no parent fees. Extracted fee data: none found.[Admissions Page][Fee/Tuition Pages]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Dreilinden-Grundschule emphasizes a **familial atmosphere** in a green, welcoming environment, fostering a sense of well-being for all students, staff, and parents through collaborative efforts.[2][8] Since the 2018/2019 school year, the school's profile focuses on **"Sport, Bewegung und Soziales"** (Sport, Movement, and Social Aspects), developed jointly by parents and teachers to integrate physical activity and social development into daily school life.[1][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Built in **1990** and fully accessible (with elevator, wheelchair-friendly toilets, and ramps), the school serves approximately **300 students** with **35 teachers** in a two-track (Zweizügigkeit) structure promoting close-knit community ties.[1][2][3] Located in Berlin's **Nikolassee** neighborhood (Steglitz-Zehlendorf district) near Wannsee, it features a large sports hall, sports field, adjacent woodland for play areas, a canteen ("Mensa") with warm meals, "Oase" social station, and computer rooms supported by the parent association "Freunde der Dreilinden-Grundschule e.V."[1][2][4][6] Historical milestones include origins in a 1939 boys' school, 1997 sports facilities modernization, 2005/06 transition to open all-day operations, and 2011 canteen opening.[6] Community involvement includes active parents, school social work, and tandem-BTL for after-school care.[1][2][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **public Grundschule (primary school, grades 1-6)**, it follows Berlin's standard curriculum with **English as the first foreign language**.[3] Key programs include **open all-day school (Offene Ganztagsschule)** from 07:30 to 13:45, offering pre- and post-class supervision, warm meals, elective sports activities (AGs), Wahlpflichtunterricht, remedial support, computer education, music school partnerships, and student newspaper.[1][2][3] Extracurriculars emphasize **sports** with diverse AGs, plus projects like U18 elections, Stolpersteine (Holocaust remembrance), recycling art, and class partnerships.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students benefit from year-group learning groups in early phases, school station support, and integrated social work via tandem-BTL for ~180 in after-school care (EFöB/Hort), including ferienbetreuung (holiday care).[1][3][4] Experiences feature class trips, school festivals, sports facilities shared with the community, and a green playground; recent updates note holiday care (e.g., Feb 2-6, 2025) and events like Fasching (Feb 17, 2025).[1][2][scraped main page] Social-pedagogical staff and "Oase" provide counseling and problem-solving.[2][5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school underwent external inspection in **2012**, evaluating teaching quality, management, and outcomes against Berlin standards (full report available on site).[5] It maintains a strong community focus with projects promoting civic engagement (e.g., elections, remembrance) and sports emphasis; no recent test results or awards detailed in sources.[1][3][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, enrollment follows Berlin public procedures; recent notice for new first-graders (Ersties) for 2026/27 school year posted Sep 26, 2025.[scraped main page] Contact sekretariat@dreilinden-grundschule.schule.berlin.de or visit during office hours (Mon-Thu 7:30-13:30, Fri 7:30-13:00); after-school care requires prior registration via tandem-BTL.[scraped main page][8]

# Dunant-Grundschule Berlin: Comprehensive School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Dunant-Grundschule embodies the values of its namesake, Henry Dunant, founder of the International Red Cross, emphasizing **humanitarian principles, community solidarity, and mutual respect**[4]. The school operates as an **"antirassistische Schule" (antiracist school)** with a clear commitment to partnership, mutual appreciation, and the well-being of all children[4].

Core pedagogical priorities include **fostering independent learning and working**, promoting **community and social interaction**, and supporting **gifted student development**[3]. The school maintains a strong focus on **integration and equal opportunity**, with children requiring special educational support taught inclusively across all grade levels[1].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Historical Background**

The Dunant-Grundschule was established in a **landmark building designed by Hans Heinrich Müller from the Gründerzeit period (1911/1912)**[1]. The school has maintained deep historical connections to its community, including relationships since 2006 with a former student who fled Nazi persecution to Palestine in 1934[4].

**Facilities and Infrastructure**

The school occupies a **protected historical building with a 1980 extension and double gymnasium**[1]. Key facilities include:

- **Library** (completely renovated in 2014)[4]
- **Two student computer labs**[4]
- **Shared campus** with the neighboring Kopernikus-Oberschule[4]
- **Cafeteria** (newly constructed; used by grades 4-6)[4]
- **Sports hall** (renovated and reopened in summer 2020 after closure since March 2018)[4]
- **Central courtyard** with playground facilities[4]

**Staffing and Community Support**

The school employs approximately **40 teachers and 17 educators**[1]. The **Freundeskreis Dunant-Schule e.V. (Friends of Dunant School)**, established in 1992, actively supports the school through funding for playground equipment, school courtyard redesign, library books, garden flowers, and musical instruments[2]. A **school station ("Ausblick")** has operated since 2001 in cooperation with youth welfare organization tandem BQG, providing social work support and training fifth-graders as "conflict mediators"[2][3].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Structure and Enrollment**

The school serves **grades 1-6** with approximately **540-566 students organized into 24 classes** across three to four parallel streams[1][3]. Classes are organized by grade level (not mixed-age) since the 2013/14 school year[2].

**Language Programs**

Students choose between **English or French as their first foreign language beginning in grade 3**[1]. This choice is made during school enrollment[1].

**Music Emphasis**

The school has a **distinct music focus** ("Musikbetont")[1]. Beginning in grade 1, students have the opportunity to learn **string instruments** through dedicated music instruction[4]. The school supports numerous musical offerings through the support association[2].

**Digital Learning**

As a **"model school for IT-supported learning"** within Berlin's eEducation Masterplan, the school has been **"chalk-free" since 2012/2013**, utilizing interactive smartboards in all classrooms and subject rooms[2][4].

**Special Education**

Children with special educational needs are **integrated and taught inclusively across all grade levels**[1].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**School Day Structure**

Instruction runs from **8:00 AM to 2:20 PM (14:20 Uhr)** across seven periods[1]. Classes are organized in **45-minute units**[1].

**Extended Care Options**

- **Verlässliche Halbtagsgrundschule (VHG)** – reliable half-day program[1]
- **Ergänzende Förderung und Betreuung (eFöB)** – supplementary care and support (can be booked at school enrollment)[1]

**Extracurricular Activities**

The school offers **working groups (AGs)** with topics and schedules available on the school's calendar page[1]. Recent activities documented on the website include:

- **Reading competition** (traditional annual event held in late November)[6]
- **Advent singing** (festive community event)[6]
- **Summer festival** (annual event with stage and trampoline)[6]
- **First aid courses** (for grades 3 and 5)[6]

**Community Engagement**

The school emphasizes **parental involvement** through participation in school festivals, the support association, and the parent council (Gesamtelternvertretung)[6].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Recognition and Accreditation**

The school received a **school inspection report for the 2023/2024 school year**, available on the school website[1]. The school has been designated a **model school for IT-supported learning** within Berlin's eEducation Masterplan[2].

**Recent Institutional Developments**

In the 2023/24 school year, the support association funded major upgrades to the **school library**, including new shelving and seating furniture after the library relocated to a larger space[6]. The school continues to invest in modern facilities and learning environments[6].

**Historical Significance**

The school maintains an active commitment to historical awareness and peace education, including participation in international partnership projects such as "Footsteps for Peace" (2012)[7].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Contact Information**

**Dunant-Grundschule**
Gritznerstr. 19-23
12163 Berlin Steglitz-Zehlendorf

**School Leadership:**
- Headmaster: Frau Schätzlein
- Secretary: Frau Tödtloff

**Contact Details:**
- Phone: +49 (0)30–797 42-160
- Fax: +49 (0)30-797 42-170
- Email: sekretariat@dunant.schule.berlin.de
- Office Location: Room 14, Ground Floor, Old Building, left entrance

**Office Hours:**
- Monday–Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM
- Friday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM

**Enrollment Process**

Parents can register their children for admission through the school's website[6]. **Supplementary care (eFöB) can be booked simultaneously with school enrollment**[1]. The school welcomes approximately **91 new first-graders annually**, as evidenced by recent enrollment ceremonies[6].

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Ebereschen-Grundschule emphasizes child protection, social learning, and inclusive education, developing a school-specific concept to prevent child endangerment from sexual abuse, violence, and bullying through team efforts, questionnaires, and parent involvement.[7] It prioritizes a supportive environment with social-pedagogical offers like counseling, conflict mediation, and group development to foster social competencies alongside academic growth.[2][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established in 1983 in northern Marzahn near Berlin's city border, the school serves a diverse community in a "Soziale Stadt" program area since 1999, with about 341-350 students, 56% from non-German speaking families and 55% qualifying for learning material exemptions.[1][2][3] Facilities include 17 classrooms across three floors, two gymnasiums, an open all-day area (eFöB) from ground floor to first upper floor, specialized rooms like construction, movement, craft, question mark, and clothing rooms, plus a school garden, reading kitchen, computer room with 18 workstations, projectors, relaxation spaces, and a water play area; the building is wheelchair accessible with ramps.[1][3][5] Contact: Borkheider Str. 28, 12689 Berlin-Marzahn; Tel: +49 030 9309511; Fax: +49 030 93497955; Email: 10G05@10G05.schule.berlin.de or ebereschen-gs@hotmail.de; Principal: Dr. Antje Stache (or Herr Fromm per some sources).[2][5][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public primary school (Grundschule) with open all-day operations (7:30-13:30 core, extendable via Hort aftercare), it offers a standard branch (mixed grades 1-3, separate from grade 4) and a Montessori branch (mixed 1-3 and 4-6), English as first foreign language, Schulanfangsphase with cross-grade groups, and inclusive support via projects like Klassencoach and Übergang.[1][3][5] Programs include school social work (counseling, social learning, class councils, peer mediation), Lernförderung, ergänzende Förderung und Betreuung (eFöB), holiday care, meals, and multiprofessional teams with special educators.[2][3][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse activities like Arbeitsgemeinschaften (keyboard, dance, judo, tae kwon do, gymnastics, basketball, karate, football, girls' sports, Russian, music school), projects, sports, movement, Hausaufgaben support, school garden, reading kitchen, excursions, and social initiatives like Lesepaten and toy lending.[1][2][5][7] Open Ganztagsbetrieb includes free-use rooms for play and creativity, with after-school Hort and ferienbetreuung.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school receives a rating of note 2 (good) for its website and operations, participates in institutional child protection enhancements, and maintains partnerships with DRK, JAO, youth office, police, hospital, and counseling centers for comprehensive support.[1][2][3][7] It accommodates ~350 diverse students effectively through social and inclusive programs, though specific academic outcomes like inspection reports are referenced but not detailed here.[8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Enrollment (Schulanmeldung) is handled via Berlin's public system for the local catchment area, with an infopoint for parents and support resources listed; no fees as a tuition-free public school.[7]

# École Voltaire Berlin - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

**École Voltaire** is a **bilingual German-French primary school** operating under the direct management of the AEFE (Agence pour l'Enseignement du Français à l'Étranger)[6]. The school's motto, "Aime la vérité mais pardonne à l'erreur" (Love truth but forgive error), reflects its educational values[8].

The school is **state-recognized as a full-day substitute school** (anerkannte Ersatzschule) and is officially accredited by the French Ministry of National Education[5]. It welcomes French and international children seeking a French education in a multilingual environment[3].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Location**: École Voltaire opened in 1994 following the withdrawal of French forces from Germany, initially in the Reinickendorf district (north Berlin) as Voltaire College[3]. The school **relocated to Tiergarten** at Kurfürstenstraße 53, 10785 Berlin, positioning itself near the Französisches Gymnasium in a central urban location[4].

**Current Enrollment**: The school serves **420 students** aged 3 to 11 (from preschool through 6th grade)[6].

**Facilities**: A new **canteen (Kantine)** was constructed as a complement to the existing building, featuring a 200-square-meter dining hall with capacity for over 120 children, designed with sustainable wooden cladding and integrated into the school's green courtyard and sports facilities[4].

**Leadership**: The school is directed by Emeline Bury[2].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels Offered**: The school operates two divisions:
- **Maternelle (Preschool)**: Ages 3-5
- **Élémentaire (Primary/Elementary)**: Grades CP through 6ème (ages 6-11)[1][2]

**Languages**: 
- **Primary language**: French[3]
- **Languages studied**: French, German, and English—three languages are integrated into the curriculum[3]
- German instruction is provided for German language and subject content (Sachkunde, Mathematik, Deutsch, Sport)[5]

**Educational Model**: The school operates as a **bilingual German-French institution** with state recognition as a full-day school[5].

**Curriculum Features**:
- Emphasis on multilingual education and cultural exchange
- Integration with Berlin institutions and cultural organizations
- Discovery classes and educational outings[3]
- Project-based learning (documented through student productions including short films, photography contests, and multilingual storytelling competitions)[7]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities and Events**:
- **Solidarity initiatives**: Annual "Spendenlauf" (charity run) benefiting the German Red Cross[7]
- **Cultural programs**: "Semaine des Langues" (Language Week) celebrating French and multilingual communication[7]
- **Arts and literature**: Visits from youth authors and illustrators; participation in multilingual storytelling contests (Kamilala competition)[7]
- **STEM engagement**: Mathematics month programming[7]
- **Media literacy**: Student-run radio station "VIP"[7]
- **Environmental education**: Eco-delegate projects promoting school sustainability[7]
- **Conferences and enrichment**: Guest speakers including astronomers and planetologists[7]

**Preschool Activities**: Dedicated "Semaine de la maternelle" (Preschool Week) programming[7]

**Friday Childcare**: "Freitagsbetreuung" (Friday after-school care) available[7]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Accreditations and Recognition**:
- **Homologated by the French Ministry of National Education** (AEFE Code: 025O13; School Number: 01P47)[2]
- **State-recognized substitute school** (anerkannte Ersatzschule) as of September 2024[5]
- Part of the AEFE network, Zone ZECO[7]

**Student Achievements**:
- Students have won awards in multilingual storytelling competitions (Kamilala Deutschland)[7]
- Recognition in photography contests ("Berlin autrement")[7]

**Community Recognition**: The school is described as practicing "excellence" (l'excellence) according to parent reviews[3], with an average rating of 4.40/5[3].

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Enrollment Process**: The school accepts applications for enrollment. An open house ("Journée Portes Ouvertes / Tag der offenen Tür") was scheduled for January 17, 2026[7].

**Admission Requirements**: The school is open to French and international children seeking French education[3].

### Fee Structure (2025-2026 Academic Year)

**Registration/Enrollment Fees** (one-time, non-refundable)[6]:
- **First child (oldest sibling)**: **€400**
- **Subsequent children**: **€200**
- Former students returning to the school are exempt from registration fees

**Annual Tuition** (paid over 10 months, September-June)[6]:
- **Maternelle (Preschool)**: **€5,350 per year**
- **Élémentaire and 6ème (Grades CP-6)**: **€5,540 per year**

**Tuition Reductions (Dégressivité)** - Applied to annual fees for CP through 6ème only[6]:
- **2nd child**: 25% reduction on annual tuition
- **3rd child and subsequent children**: 40% reduction on annual tuition

**Income-Based Tuition Reduction (Dégressivité)**: Families whose income does not exceed a certain threshold may request a degressive reduction of tuition fees. This applies only to elementary and 6ème students. Families receiving employer-sponsored tuition assistance or family allowances are ineligible. Requests should be submitted to the school's financial office (Layla LAFDAL - layla.lafdal@ecolevoltaire.de)[6].

**Meal Plan Costs**:
- Managed by external provider Forum Esswirtschaft, Leckerlogisch
- **Elementary students**: Subsidized by Berlin Senate (since January 2021)
- **Preschool students**: Not subsidized
- **Packed lunch option**: €100 per year (billed quarterly: €40, €30, €30) for families bringing cold meals[6]

**Additional Fees**:
- School trips and outings may incur additional charges depending on teacher-led projects[6]

**Scholarships**: French nationals registered with the French Consulate in Berlin may apply for school scholarships under certain conditions. Applications are processed by the Consulate and must be submitted online via https://scolaide.aefe.gouv.fr/ (as of February 2025)[6].

**Payment Method**: Invoices are no longer sent by postal mail. Families access and manage billing through the school's parent portal (SKOLENGO platform): https://ev-berlin.family-administration.skolengo.net/connexion[6].

# Edison-Grundschule (09G21) School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Edison-Grundschule is guided by the motto of its namesake, Thomas Alva Edison: "If there is a way to do something better, find it."[3] The school positions itself as **an inclusive institution for all students with diverse needs**, operating as a place for shared learning, living, discovering, researching, well-being, and play.[3]

Core educational principles include:[3]

- **Social learning as foundational**: Social competence is prioritized as a prerequisite for all forms of learning
- **Democratic culture**: The school emphasizes respect, community, and mutual acceptance
- **Individualized development**: Recognition and strengthening of each student's individuality and personality
- **Collaborative learning**: Students learn both with and from one another, leveraging diversity as a learning resource

The school offers learning in both **age-homogeneous and mixed-age groups spanning grades 1-6**.[3]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Infrastructure**

The Edison-Grundschule is located in Oberschöneweide, a district within Treptow-Köpenick in Berlin.[1][2] The school comprises **two connected school buildings** sharing a common campus, with a gymnasium also on the grounds.[1]

**Outdoor Facilities**[1]

The spacious schoolyard includes:
- Climbing equipment
- Sports field with soccer goals and basketball hoops
- Running track
- Gymnastics and balance equipment
- Vegetable gardens (Hochbeete) providing nature access
- Adjacent sports field from the Oberstufenzentrum (available for grades 4-6 during breaks and afternoons)
- Proximity to the Wuhlheide nature area

**Indoor Facilities**[1]

The old building (Altbau) houses:
- Classrooms for grades 1-4
- Music room
- Computer room
- Pottery workshop (Töpferkeller)
- Woodworking workshop
- School station (Schulstation)
- Band room
- Administrative offices

The new building (Neubau) contains:
- Classrooms for grades 5-6 and mixed-age groups (Jül 4-6)
- Modern science laboratory[1]

**Staffing**[4]

As of the 2018 school inspection:
- 44 teachers
- 16 educators (Erzieherinnen und Erzieher)
- 1 school social worker
- 1 secretary
- 1 school caretaker

**Student Population**[8]

The school currently serves **460 students** (51% female, 49% male) across grades 1-6:
- Grade 1: 55 students
- Grade 2: 64 students
- Grade 3 (including mixed-age groups): 122 students
- Grade 4: 44 students
- Grade 5: 40 students

- Cooperative learning structures

Lunch is provided through **Sodexo**.

- Goal-oriented school leadership involving all stakeholder groups in school development

**Areas for Development** identified included:

The school serves as a **Hospitationsschule** (demonstration school for professional visits).

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Eduard-Mörike-Schule emphasizes a multifaceted school life inspired by its namesake, an evangelical pastor known for writing stories, fairy tales, poems, translation, and teaching Latin, promoting diversity in daily activities like lessons, breaks, extracurriculars, partnerships, and events.[Main Page][About Page] Core ideas include school as a place for growing, learning, working, and living with space for individuality and community; mutual respect, trust, tolerance, and conscious use of German for communication; holistic learning from real-life experiences; individualized support tailored to students' interests and abilities; parent collaboration; openness to the neighborhood; and values education through social learning to resolve conflicts peacefully.[About Page]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1904, the school serves approximately 315 students across 16 classes in Berlin-Neukölln, supported by 28 teachers (many from diverse countries, with three focused on intensive German language acquisition for non-native speakers), 17 educators, 3 social workers, a special education pedagogue, secretary, janitor, and kitchen team—totaling around 60 adults.[2] The schoolyard was fully redesigned in 2019, providing play and seating areas.[2] Facilities include a computer lab, school library, student club, wheelchair-accessible restrooms, whiteboards in all classes, a learning workshop, and a newly set up parents' café.[1][6] It offers bound full-day operations (GGB) and year-group learning groups in the early phase, with partnerships like LebensWelt gGmbH since 2009 for programs including a mobile student club.[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public primary school (Grundschule, school number 08G18), it introduces English as the first foreign language and follows Berlin's framework curricula with emphasis on individualization, differentiation, and language development.[1][2] Programs include intensive German instruction in small groups for non-German speakers, social learning to teach conflict resolution without insults or violence, and support from a special education specialist.[About Page][2] Extracurricular working groups (AGs) cover badminton, gardening, mural painting, book workshop, theater play, graffiti, film/video, cheerleading, guitar, kiosk management, soccer (with tournaments), and play/sports.[1][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in a supportive environment fostering autonomy, real-world experiential learning, and social skills, with full-day care including meals and after-school activities via the mobile student club open to other schools' children.[1][5] Experiences include development talks, sports events like volleyball and soccer tournaments (e.g., EMS Völkerballturnier on 11.12.2025, Fußball Sparki-Cup on 28.01.2026), and community events amid school holidays like Christmas (22.12.2025–02.01.2026) and winter breaks (02.02.2026–06.02.2026).[Main Page] Parental involvement and neighborhood partnerships enhance holistic growth.[About Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school takes pride in rapid language progress for non-native speakers through small-group intensive German classes.[2] It participates in local sports cups and maintains a collaborative staff model with joint projects and conferences for consistent educational goals.[2] Recent infrastructure upgrades like the 2019 schoolyard and parents' café support community engagement, though specific academic outcomes or awards are not detailed in available content.[2][6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule, admissions follow Berlin's standard enrollment process for local residents, with an open day listed in the school calendar; no specific fees or selective criteria are mentioned.[School Information][1]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Eichendorff-Grundschule aims to provide approximately 450 students in age-homogeneous classes with a happy school experience in a familiar atmosphere during their entry into school life.[1][2] Key focuses include promoting reading competence as a "Lesende Schule," violence prevention, pedagogical school development, philosophizing, and research.[1] The school positions itself as an open, diverse neighborhood school ("Kiezschule") that supports children from varied cultural backgrounds together into the future.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1867 as a boys' city school and later the first community school in Charlottenburg, the school has been named after Romantic poet Joseph Freiherr von Eichendorff since 1954; its current building in Goethestraße was constructed in 1972-73 on the site of WWII-destroyed structures.[1][3][4] Located in Berlin-Charlottenburg's Karl-August-Kiez within a traffic-calmed area of Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district, it serves a socio-culturally heterogeneous catchment area between Kaiser-Friedrich-, Bismarck-, Schlüter-, and Kantstraßen, with around 420-450 students.[1][2][5] Facilities include expansive outdoor areas with a playground, sports fields, play and climbing landscapes, green spaces with mature trees, a quiet inner courtyard, an integrated after-school care (Hort), a freestanding sports hall, a multi-purpose hall with stage, library, computer-equipped classrooms, computer room, workshop with ceramics kiln, and a blue column monument honoring Eichendorff.[1][3][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school operates as a public **Grundschule** (primary school, grades 1-4/6) with age-homogeneous classes, open all-day care (OGB), and a school entry phase with year-group learning groups; English is taught as the first foreign language.[1][6] Instructional development occurs through "Professional Learning Communities," with varied methods like virtual shopping in math or creative projects in German based on self-chosen readings.[5] Special emphases include reading promotion, violence prevention, philosophical inquiry, research, and pedagogical development; after-school care educators support classes up to 8 hours weekly in grades 1-3.[1][5] Extracurricular activities (AGs) encompass bauchtanz, capoeira, English, French (grade 2), football, guitar, Italian, oboe, drums, Serbian, dancing, and Turkish.[6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience a supportive environment with high work readiness, friendly interactions, individual help, and teacher feedback; the engaged team of teachers and educators emphasizes continuous training and enjoyment in working with children.[5][9] Facilities enable play, exploration, hide-and-seek, and nature observation beyond lessons; partnerships include local kindergartens, the Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf music school, sports providers, and school social work since 2021/22.[2][5][6] Recent events feature open doors, parent info evenings, flea markets, student office hours, and a parents' association WhatsApp channel.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
An inspection highlights strengths in leadership driving school and instructional development, an engaged staff implementing priorities, targeted quality cycles, and participation of parents and students.[5] The principal actively advances development, with professional learning communities fostering varied, goal-oriented instruction.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Information on enrollment and school entry ("Anmeldung und Einschulung") and transition to secondary school ("Übergang in die Oberschule") is available on the website; upcoming events include an open day and parent info evening on September 24, 2025, and a school year 2025/2026 planner.[1] Contact the secretariat at sekretariat@eichendorff.schule.berlin.de or +49 30 437272270.[6][8]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Elbe-Schule is a public primary school (Grundschule) emphasizing communal learning, personal development, and social interaction to foster confident, linguistically competent, creative, and responsible individuals.[3][5] Its four central pillars include living democracy and taking responsibility through violence-free, tolerant, and participatory practices; language as a key to the world via systematic speech and reading promotion across all subjects; art as personal expression through music, visual arts, theater, and dance; and educational partnerships with families based on openness and mutual respect.[3] The school profiles as art-oriented (kunstbetont), integrating social learning, democracy education, exploratory learning, and continuous language development to educate tolerant, democratic citizens in a multicultural context.[1][2][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located at Elbestrasse 11, 12045 Berlin (Neukölln), the Elbe-Schule (school number 08G05) serves approximately **250 students** with **35 teachers**, **15 educators**, and **3 school social workers** in a densely built 19th-century neighborhood with a predominantly multicultural, low-income community (over 80% of parents exempt from learning material contributions).[4][5][6] Led by principal **D. Deniz Taner** (full name Devrim Deniz Taner), with deputy **Fatih Özden** and other key staff including social workers and a caretaker, it operates as an open all-day school (offene Ganztagsschule) from 6-18:00 with modular supplementary care.[1][4][5][7] Facilities include a school garden for ecology, sustainability, responsibility, teamwork, and healthy eating; two gym halls, computer rooms, subject rooms, teaching kitchen, library, parents' center, bike AG with school bikes, and recent expansion plans for a three-track structure and aula since 2018.[1][4][5][9] Partnerships involve local education networks, Aspe e.V., Lions Club, and initiatives like parents' café and fathers' meetings.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICURUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum integrates a strong **art-focused profile** (kunstbetontes Profil) with intensive art education supporting creativity, language, and communication; music and theater for community experiences; and cross-cutting language/reading promotion, including for non-German speakers in welcome classes and LRS (reading-writing weakness) groups.[1][2][3][4][6] Democracy education features workshops, student parliament, and projects emphasizing values like freedom, equality, and participation via project-based learning, debates, and cooperative methods.[3][5] Additional programs include English as first foreign language, swimming for 2nd grade, schulgarden-based biology/environmental education, and all-day schedule up to 16:00 with elective hours.[4][5][7] Special offers: artistic workshops, exhibitions, project days, AGs (bike, sports, theater/music, chess, cooking/sports, healthy eating, inline skating, student newspaper), reading pros, Gripsfieber theater, and conflict management like buddy projects and cool kids programs.[1][2][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in hands-on, participatory activities like school garden projects promoting sustainability and teamwork, sports tournaments (e.g., volleyball on 27.01.26 with fair play emphasis, futsal on 22.01.26), peace initiatives (e.g., balloon releases and "99 Luftballons" project from 2024), career trips (e.g., 6b to Zapf Umzüge logistics firm), and student parliament meetings for co-determination.[3][5] Extracurriculars foster inclusion via student newspaper AG with idea box, theater/music projects, and social learning (weekly hour on social learning/democracy).[1][3] Meals provided by Apetito; inclusive for 12 students with special needs; open atmosphere encourages idea-sharing in a multikulturell setting with low home support.[4][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school maintains a revised program since 2015 highlighting art profile, violence prevention, exploratory learning, and parent cooperation, with art visibly integrated school-wide and positive inspection notes on creativity and cultural engagement despite needs in activating methods and integration.[6][8] Recent events showcase fair play and community (e.g., tournament winners: 4b, 6b/5b), ongoing democracy via active parliament, and projects like peace symbols and career outings indicating strong social-emotional growth.[3] Offers leseförderung, LRS-kurses, and homework help; prepares for 21st-century challenges through critical thinking and participation.[3][4][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school** (öffentlich), admission follows Berlin public primary school processes; open all-day with supplementary care modules from 6-18:00 at Elbestrasse 11 (pavilion onsite).[1][4] Invites visits via information events and open house (Tag der offenen Tür) to experience the community; contact sekretariat@08g05.schule.berlin.de or +49 30 632250880.[3][4][5] Parking for disabled available; year-group learning groups in early phase.[4]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Elisabeth-Christinen-Grundschule (ECG) emphasizes individualized student support, intercultural encounters, and cultural education as a unifying element for inclusion and heterogeneity. It fosters a respectful community that values diversity, promotes emotional, social, intellectual, and intercultural development, and creates a welcoming learning environment through varied teaching methods like open, structured, and project-based work.[2] The school views itself as a "Kiez" (neighborhood) school for all, with cultural education in music, theater, and visual arts connecting diverse participants.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after Elisabeth Christine, wife of Frederick II, who resided in Schloss Schönhausen, the school is located in Berlin-Pankow, Niederschönhausen.[1] It serves as a community hub with cooperative partnerships including Schloss Schönhausen and Musikschule Fröhlich.[1] Facilities include a basement cafeteria used as an aula, classrooms organized by grade levels across four floors, specialized rooms, playgrounds with hills and gardens, a paved sports area with play equipment, and access to a nearby sports hall on the Konrad-Duden-Oberschule grounds (about 50 meters away).[1] The school operates a half-day program with a social-pedagogical area managed by an external provider (tjfbg), including early/late care from 6:00-18:00.[4][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public primary school (Grundschule), ECG follows Berlin's Grundschulverordnung (GsVO): 20 weekly hours in grade 1, 21 in grade 2, and 24 in grade 3 (adding English).[2] It offers an English elective group (AG) from grade 1, primarily in grade 2, in parent cooperation.[2] Grades 5-6 feature elective profile courses in artistic, linguistic, sports, and natural sciences areas, plus Wahlpflichtunterricht for specialization.[1][5] Special focuses include gifted education via the federal "Leistung macht Schule" program (pilot school 2018-2023, now transferring knowledge) and the mBET tool; methods like differentiation, project work, cooperative learning, AGs, competitions, and external partners.[1][3] Documents cover social learning, violence prevention, bike training, and school rules.[Main Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse activities: theater visits (e.g., class 2a saw "Das hässliche Entlein"), class trips (e.g., grades 5a/5d to Feriendorf Dorado with sports and swimming), herb days (class 3b at garden school making herb salt), Berlin-Rallye (class 6a exploring the city via Bürgerstiftung project), and reading challenges (Antolin rankings, Easter challenge).[Main Page][6] Three entry classes welcomed 81 first-graders recently.[8] Staff includes leadership (Frau Jaeschke, Frau von Oven), class teachers, specialists (e.g., music/art, special ed, sports, English), welcome class teacher, and social pedagogues.[4][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
ECG participated as one of 15 Berlin pilot schools in "Leistung macht Schule" (2018-2023), developing strategies for high-achieving and potentially gifted students, supported by Berlin's gifted education office; it now aids transfer to other schools.[3] Reading successes include top Antolin scores: class 3c (30,853 points, grades 1-3) and 4c (48,999 points, grades 4-6) as of July 2025.[Main Page] Extracurricular wins feature in city projects like Berlin-Rallye.[6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule, enrollment is local and mandatory for eligible ages. For 2026/27, registration occurs Friday, 17.10.2025 (8:00-10:00), requiring parent's ID, child's birth certificate, measles vaccination proof, custody documents, info sheet, and enrollment form; no registration outside hours.[6] Secretariat hours: Mon-Fri 08-10 and 11:30-13; contact +49 30 4005 8920 or sekretariat@ecg.schule.[Main Page] Documents include enrollment info sheet for 26/27.[Main Page]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Elisabethstift-Schule operates as a state-recognized **Gemeinschaftsschule** emphasizing individual learning support, social competence strengthening, and building sustainable relationships as prerequisites for academic learning.[1][2] It follows principles of **existential pedagogy** grounded in Christian values, viewing school as a learning and living space for all involved.[1][2] Education is inclusive, supporting students with and without special needs, including "non-regularly schoolable" children, in small classes of maximum 12 students per class taught by a fixed team of one teacher and one pedagogue.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2008 as a Grundschule, the school expanded to a Gemeinschaftsschule in 2014 and merged with a special education support center in 2020 to enhance inclusive instruction while maintaining small class sizes.[2] Located in Berlin-Hermsdorf at Falkentaler Steig 10, 13467 Berlin, it is part of the Elisabethstift Berlin's work in free sponsorship (private school).[1][2] It fosters a personal and open atmosphere among students and staff.[2]

### 3. CURRICURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school serves **classes 1-10** (Grundschule through Sekundarstufe), adhering to Berlin's state curriculum framework while prioritizing social and personal competencies, interdisciplinary, and life-relevant content with individual, experimental learning.[1][2] It offers an open all-day program with supplementary support and care for classes 1-6, and a structured all-day program for classes 7-10.[1][2] No specific languages, special programs, or graduation pathways like Abitur are detailed.[1][2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
All-day operations include varied afternoon activities (AG-Angebot) from 14:00-16:00, such as soccer, judo, cooking, and computer classes, for supplementary support.[2] The focus on inclusion and small teams supports personalized experiences, but no additional extracurriculars, sports, arts, or exchanges are specified.[1][2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, or specific student outcomes are mentioned in the available content.[1][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve downloading and submitting an application form by mail to Falkentaler Steig 10, 13467 Berlin (stichwort: SJ 2026/27), followed by a one-hour interview with the child and parents led by pedagogical leadership to assess school readiness, especially for class 1.[6] Interviews for class 1 conclude by end of January 2026, for class 7 by mid-March 2026, with written decisions afterward; placement depends on available spots.[6]  
**Fees**: A monthly **Schulgeld** (school fee) is charged, as typical for private schools, and is **income-dependent** (einkommensabhängig gestaffelt); no specific EUR amounts, ranges, or other fees (e.g., registration, materials, meals, after-school care) are provided.[2] Supplementary support and care costs are calculated by the responsible district office.[2]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Elizabeth-Shaw-Grundschule is an open all-day primary school (offene Ganztagsgrundschule) with a **music-focused profile** (musikbetontes Profil), emphasizing music, art, and literature as central elements.[2][1] Its guiding principles (Leitbild) promote curiosity and joy in learning, effort and perseverance, aesthetic learning environments, mutual trust, sense of community through traditions, and polite interactions; the school integrates musical elements systematically across school life and teaching as part of the "Musikalische Grundschule" certification project.[2][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after author and illustrator **Elizabeth Shaw** (1920–2004), who conveyed values like tolerance, respect, empathy, and responsibility through her children's books without didacticism, the school honors her via annual "Elizabeth-Shaw-Tag," integration of her works in German, music, and art classes, and library focus on her books.[6][3][2] Historical roots trace to late 18th-century Pankow village school; by 1887, it served 586 children in 9 classes, later splitting by gender before modern consolidation at Grunowstr. 17, 13187 Berlin-Pankow, with buildings named Bärenhaus (main), Fuchsbau (modular), and Hasenhaus (temporary).[3][7][1] Community partnerships include Musikschule Pankow Béla Bartók, Janusz-Korczak-Bibliothek, Bertelsmann Stiftung, and Polizeiabschnitt 13; facilities support open all-day care (Offene Ganztagbetreuung/OGB) from 07:00–17:00, student library, and traffic education at Jugendverkehrsschule Pankow, though past inspections noted spatial constraints for dual classroom use.[1][3][4] Located near S+U Pankow station.[9]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Follows Berlin primary school regulations with core subjects **Deutsch, Mathematik, Kunst, Musik, Sport** (including mandatory swimming in grade 3, optional in grade 2, and recorder in grades 1–2 at half-class size) across grades 1–6; Sachunterricht (grades 1–4) differentiates into Natur- und Gesellschaftswissenschaften (grades 5–6); **Englisch** or **Französisch** from grade 3; grades 5–6 include customizable **Schwerpunktbildung**.[5][1] Music emphasis via Flötenunterricht, partnerships, and certification; pedagogical structure features 3–4 parallel classes, year-group learning groups in early phases, life-world relevance, hands-on materials, differentiation, language development, self-reliance, and traffic education.[3][1] Supplementary Förderunterricht in small groups and special needs support for learning.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience music-integrated daily routines (school starts 7:55), all-day care including Hort (07:00–17:00) and VHG (reliable half-day to 13:30), working groups (AGs) like choir, blockflöte, querflöte, judo, creative dance, guitar, volleyball, drums, computer; events include "Große lesen für Kleine" (prominents/parents reading), Vorlesewettbewerb (reading competition qualifying for Berlin-wide), Elizabeth-Shaw-Tag, and library use.[2][1][3][5] Past inspections (2000s) highlighted stimulating, motivating lessons with positive social climate, high satisfaction, cooperative leadership, and reading evaluations, fostering well-being despite spatial limits.[4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified for systematic "Musikalische Grundschule" development with qualified music staff and quality criteria met; strong evaluation practices for transparency in subjects; positive inspection outcomes including high participant satisfaction, supportive social climate, transparent leadership, and engaging learning environments.[2][4] Offers like OGB, music profile, and AGs enhance holistic development; student reading successes in competitions.[2][1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public Grundschule** (tuition-free), admissions follow Berlin's eGovernment Schulanmeldung process; open all-day structure with early-phase year-group groups and individual support.[1][3][10]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Ellef-Ringnes-Grundschule operates as an educational and learning community inspired by Norwegian philanthropist Ellef Ringnes, emphasizing mutual support, respect for diversity in people, things, and the environment.[2][3] Key principles include supporting individual learning paths to foster curiosity and joy in learning, recognizing personal achievements and social behavior, promoting independence and self-confidence, encouraging healthy lifestyles in harmony with nature, and maintaining open collaboration with parents while providing a protected space for proactive child welfare.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in the Heiligensee district of Berlin, the school is named after Ellef Ringnes for his contributions to youth education and well-being, featuring high teaching quality, engaged staff, diverse offerings, and vibrant school life.[3] Facilities include well-equipped classrooms with interactive whiteboards, a computer room with 28 workstations (also noted as 20 in official profiles), a library, two gymnasiums (Sporthalle), outdoor sports and play areas, accessible features like parking for disabled persons, wheelchair ramps, elevators, and toilets, plus a canteen (Mensa) and leisure rooms for after-school care.[1][3][7] Leadership includes Schulleiter Jens Albrecht and Konrektorin Trenner, supported by a team of teachers, after-school educators (Hortteam), administrative staff, social workers, and a school dog named Nala.[1][5] The school promotes community through parent involvement and projects like "Haus der kleinen Forscher" (pilot school for young researchers) and "lesende Schule" (reading school).[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public German Grundschule (primary school), the curriculum includes English as the first foreign language, learning groups for reading and writing difficulties, year-group-based groups in the early school phase, and open all-day care (Offene Ganztagbetreuung, OGB).[1] Focus areas highlighted are **digitale Bildung** (digital education), **Grüne Schule** (green school), **SchulLEBEN** (school life), and **Theater und Schule** (theater and school), with modern tech integration like interactive whiteboards and classroom media islands.[1][3] Unterrichtszeiten (lesson times) are structured but specific schedules are not detailed in available content.[scraped]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in a rich school life with projects, celebrations, sports, and cultural events, including theater, circus, vegetable garden (Gemüseacker), TUSCH, and a school dog concept.[3][5][8] Extracurriculars feature basketball AG (after-school group) advancing to higher leagues, annual Tanzprojekt (dance project) drawing 1000 guests, student parliament elections for representatives and budgets, ferienprogramm (holiday programs), and after-school care with meals.[scraped][1][9] Emphasis on holistic development includes healthy living, nature harmony, and social skills in a supportive environment.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recent highlights include the 19th Tanzprojekt on January 29, 2026, sold out with 1000 guests; girls' basketball team securing 3rd place in Grundschulliga (from 5 teams), qualifying for a higher league; and student parliament elections completed pre-holidays.[scraped] Certifications and projects include "Haus der kleinen Forscher," "lesende Schule," and ongoing SPI program participation since August 2021.[1][4] The school maintains strong infrastructure and accessibility, supporting broad learner success.[1][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule in Berlin (Schulnummer 12G17), admissions follow standard Berlin public school enrollment via eGovernment Schulanmeldung processes, with no fees or specific requirements detailed beyond general eligibility for local primary students.[1][ownership] Contact sekretariat@erg.schule.berlin.de or (030) 41 74 40 780 for inquiries.[3]
