### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Grundschule am Windmühlenberg emphasizes a child-oriented, hands-on learning approach in an open all-day program, fostering individualized education from the school entry phase with year-group-spanning classes (Jahrgangsübergreifendes Lernen, JüL) starting in grade 1.[3][1] It promotes a nurturing environment "Geborgen im Grünen groß werden" (growing up safely in the green), supported by projects like "Gesunde Schule" (healthy school), musical education as a "Musikalische Grundschule," and community involvement quoting “Um ein Kind zu erziehen, braucht es ein ganzes Dorf” (It takes a whole village to raise a child).[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded with roots in 1839 when school lessons began in Gatow, the school building was originally planned as a lazaretto but became a school post-WWII; it was named "Schule am Windmühlenberg – Grundschule" in 1957, became independent in 1971 as Spandau's 24th primary school, added integration classes in 1992, and expanded with a 1985 annex.[6][10] Located in Berlin's Spandau district (Gatow, near Havel and Rieselfelder) at Am Kinderdorf 23, 14089 Berlin, it serves 399-418 students in 12 classes of 20-26 pupils, led by principal Susanne Schäfer with 22 teachers and 12 educators.[1][2][3][5] Facilities include a large grass playground with a versatile play area, artificial turf sports field, grass soccer field with long jump, and an outdoor stage; it partners with "Havel-Kids" for reliable half-day (VHG) and extended care (eFöB).[1][3] Community ties include a parent association (GEV), support group (Förderverein) raising €1200 for orangutan sponsorship, student parliament visited by officials like Staatssekretärin Lilian Tschan, and Ukrainian welcome classes.[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a two-track primary school (Grundschule, grades 1-6), it offers year-group-spanning learning in the entry phase (SAPh) since 2008/09 with four groups, focusing on action- and child-oriented instruction tailored to individual needs.[3] Specialized departments cover art, math, social studies (Ge/Ek/SozW), religion, natural sciences (NaWi), German, music, English, and sports; additional programs include musical primary school, school hymn, brass class (Bläserklasse), conflict mediators (Konfliktlotsen), and senior partners.[1][3] Grading keys exist for subjects like math, sports, art, music, German, English, social sciences, and natural sciences/sachunterricht.[3] It provides free hot meals and integrates open all-day operations with VHG half-day reliability.[3][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse, project-based activities across years, such as England trips (2024/2025), upcycling (4a), math fun weeks, LandArt, butterfly projects, chick hatching, theater visits, forest days, circus projects, and nutrition workshops; recent highlights include student parliament discussions and sponsor runs.[1][3] Extracurriculars feature AGs (working groups) for 2024/25, school newspaper, green classroom, storytelling (Erzählzeit), explanatory songs, cooking ("Cooking is fun"), and outings like Tempelhofer Feld or botanical garden.[1][3] Support includes school social work, violence prevention, language development concepts, media/foray/representation plans, and integration for Ukrainian students via welcome classes with specialized staff.[3][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTSTANDING OUTCOMES
The school's VHG half-day program and "Havel-Kids" collaboration receive praise for detailed development portfolios, flexible groups, child-appropriate spaces, and staff integration into school life and events.[3] It successfully introduced free hot meals after challenges, hosts high-profile visits like Staatssekretärin Lilian Tschan (Jan 2025), and sustains traditions like orangutan sponsorship (€1200 raised via bazaar in 2026).[1][8] Enrollment demand is strong among Ukrainian families, enabling quick welcome class openings; infrastructure expansions address space needs amid stable student numbers (368-418).[2][4][6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule owned by the state, admission follows Berlin's local enrollment process for primary schools in the Gatow/Spandau area, with capacity for growth (current 1.5 Zug capacity utilization).[1][2][7] It accommodates diverse needs via JüL from grade 1, integration, Ukrainian welcome classes, and open all-day care; contact sekretariat@05g24.schule.berlin.de or +49 30 36208550 for inquiries.[4][5]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Grundschule an der Marie emphasizes fostering a sense of security, social competence, healthy self-esteem, and community belonging among students, while promoting values like respect, tolerance, and personal development.[3][1] Its core pillars include **Demokratie Lernen** (enabling children to actively participate in shaping school life, take responsibility, and experience self-efficacy), **Soziales Lernen** (developing social and emotional skills for respectful interaction, empathetic communication, cooperative work, and non-violent conflict resolution, with 20 minutes daily across all grades), and **Engagement Lernen** (encouraging responsibility for self and others, community service, and early civic engagement).[3][7] The school operates as Berlin's first official **Kinderrechteschule** (certified since 2013/14 by the Kinderhilfswerk), integrating children's rights into all activities, with a focus on "Wir fordern und fördern" (we demand and promote self-reliance, responsibility, and a conflict-free learning environment).[1][2][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established in its current form since 2001, the school building dates to 1907–1908 (designed by Ludwig Hoffmann as a listed monument) and underwent major renovations from 1994–1999 (25 million Mark investment) plus a 2013 schoolyard renewal (87,535 EUR).[2][5] Located in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg (Bezirk Pankow, Winsviertel), it serves ~480–700 students in a child-friendly, vibrant neighborhood, with strong parent-teacher collaboration via the Förderverein (funding media workshops, school newspaper, etc.).[1][2][5][6] Facilities include a fully **barrierefrei** (barrier-free) building with two elevators, accessible restrooms on all floors, a school library (managed by 5th/6th-grade Profilkurs students, offering books, magazines, audiobooks, reading corners, games like chess), school garden, theater spaces, and a renovated schoolyard with heritage paving and ramps.[2][3][4][5][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public **Ganztagsschule** (full-day school with open all-day offerings) and reliable half-day Grundschule, it features a **Schulanfangsphase (SAPh)** with jahrgangsübergreifendes Lernen (cross-grade learning groups), project-based and interdisciplinary teaching, differentiation for diverse needs, and inklusion since 1992 (supporting all special needs via a dedicated special educator for learning, language, behavior; peer groups, pools for language support).[1][2][4][6] **Profilkurse** (profile courses) in 5th/6th grades (e.g., library management) are weekly and scheduled; other programs include Verkehrshelfende (traffic safety helpers), Konfliktlotsende (conflict mediators), theater work, school newspaper, and AGs (extracurricular activities).[3][8][9] Inklusion is building-wide, enabling physical and learning disabilities integration.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in hands-on democracy via **Kinderparlament** (children's parliament), social learning through daily sessions, role-playing, and class councils; engagement via schulgarten (school garden), hort (after-school care), and community projects.[3][7] Extracurriculars like Bibliothek visits during breaks, theater, and Verkehrshelfende (7:30–7:55 AM traffic guidance) build responsibility.[3][8][9] The inclusive, barrierefrei environment supports diverse backgrounds, with emphasis on empathy, cooperation, and a "Wir-Gefühl" (we-feeling) in a conflict-arm atmosphere; ~480–700 pupils experience personalized, anspruchsvolle (challenging) offers in Prenzlauer Berg's social hub.[1][4][6][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified as Berlin's first and one of Germany's earliest **Kinderrechtsschulen** (2013/14, Kinderhilfswerk award), with a nationwide network; praised in inspections for differentiated, project-oriented teaching, inklusion, and preparation for secondary school in a heterogeneous community.[1][2][6][7] Long-term school-social work cooperation, barrierefrei status, and popularity (one of Prenzlauer Berg's most sought-after schools) highlight outcomes in social competence, self-efficacy, and teilhabe (participation) for all.[1][2][6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public Grundschule (Öffentlich), admission follows Berlin's standard catchment-area process for local residents; no specific fees or selective criteria mentioned. Contact: Sekretariat (Frau Montes Garcia), Schulleitung (Herr Beyer), +49 30 246 37... (full details on site).[10] Inklusion open to all special needs since 1992.[4]

# Grundschule an der Peckwisch - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

**Grundschule an der Peckwisch** is a **public elementary school** dedicated to serving all children as a learning and living space that reflects the diversity of contemporary society[2]. The school's core philosophy centers on the motto **"Vielfalt macht Spaß - Miteinander lernen, miteinander leben!"** (Diversity is fun - Learning together, living together!)[2].

The school embraces **inclusive education** with a commitment to fostering respect and cooperation across cultural and social differences. Children from over 25 nations learn together, and the pedagogical team reflects this same diversity in both background and training[2]. The school holds the designation **"Schule ohne Rassismus - Schule mit Courage"** (School Without Racism - School With Courage), demonstrating its commitment to anti-racism and social responsibility[2].

Rather than pursuing a single institutional profile, the school intentionally offers **diverse educational approaches** to meet the varied needs and strengths of all learners[2].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Contact:**
- Address: Tornower Weg 26-34, 13439 Berlin (Märkisches Viertel district)[4][5]
- Phone: +49 30 41508006[4][5]
- Email: schulleitung@peckwisch.de[4]
- Website: www.peckwisch.de[3]
- School Number: 12G09[3]
- Leadership: Andreas Aufderhaar (Schulleitung)[5]

**Facilities and Capacity:**
The school operates as a **reliable half-day primary school with open all-day care (offener Ganztagsbetrieb)**[2]. A recent expansion increased school capacity to **560 places** through the addition of classroom spaces, care rooms, and a cafeteria[6].

**Community Partnerships:**
The school operates under the **ALEP e.V. organization** as its carrier[3]. Since September 2009, the school has hosted a **school-based youth social work program** ("Schulbezogene Jugendsozialarbeit") at the Schülerinsel (Student Island), providing preventive support services[1].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Learning Organization:**
Teaching is organized **by grade level (jahrgangsbezogen)** across all class levels[2]. The school emphasizes fostering children's natural curiosity through **project-oriented instruction** and a balance between structured, systematic learning and open work forms including free work and group activities[2].

**Technology Integration:**
Many instructional units are delivered **multimedially using Activboards** and computer labs[2].

**Foreign Language:**
**English** is the primary foreign language offered[5].

**Extracurricular Activities (AGs):**
The school provides a **rich range of working groups** including[2]:
- Basketball (in cooperation with Alba)
- Building/Construction
- Art
- School Garden
- Singing
- Dancing

Additional specialized programs include **dance AGs** offered in partnership with the youth art school ATRIUM[7].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**School-Based Youth Social Work:**
The school offers comprehensive **preventive social work services** addressing student wellbeing[1]:

**For Students:**
- Open offerings: break-time activities, projects, creative and movement-oriented AGs
- Individual case support: counseling for peer conflicts, teacher relationships, family problems, school and test anxiety; competency training; grade support services
- Group social work: social learning, constructive conflict resolution, peer mediation, prevention projects, team development activities[1]

**For Families:**
- Information and counseling on family and parenting questions
- Support during stress, conflict, and crisis situations
- Child protection guidance
- Facilitation of conversations with specialists
- Mediation and accompaniment to specialized counseling and therapy services[1]

All services operate under **strict confidentiality and legal professional secrecy**[1].

**Student Representation:**
The **Student Council (Schülervertretung)** is a central governance body where elected class representatives from grades 3-6 meet monthly to discuss school matters and propose improvements. The council undertakes two project trips annually, fostering democratic participation and civic responsibility[2].

**Cultural and Artistic Engagement:**
The school actively integrates arts education, including partnerships with organizations like **Ephra** for art mediation projects that connect childhood and artistic expression[7].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not provide specific standardized test scores, academic rankings, or quantified achievement metrics. However, the school demonstrates commitment to **quality improvement** through ongoing facility upgrades and active student engagement in school governance and cultural projects[7].

The school's **65-year history** (celebrated in the 2021/2022 school year) indicates sustained community trust and institutional stability[8].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

The search results do not contain specific information about admissions procedures, enrollment requirements, or application deadlines. As a **public school in Berlin**, it operates under the city's public school enrollment system. Prospective families should contact the school directly at **030-41508006** or **schulleitung@peckwisch.de** for enrollment information.

The school offers **open all-day care (EFöB/Hort)** as part of its extended services[2][5].

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto is **"Fit für die Zukunft"** (Fit for the Future), emphasizing preparation for the future through innovative programs like **"Tanzende Schule"** (Dancing School), media education (**Medienbildung**), conflict mediation training (**Streitschlichterausbildung**), and the project **"Gewaltfrei Lernen"** (Violence-Free Learning).[1][8]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Spandau's Haselhorst district at Grützmacherweg 7, 13599 Berlin, the public primary school (**Grundschule**) serves 677 students (as of September 27, 2024).[1][2][5] It operates as an **offene Ganztagsgrundschule (OGB)** with after-school care (**Hort Goldmühle**) provided by Evangelische Johannesstift Jugendhilfe gGmbH at Goldbeckweg 21.[1][5][7] Facilities include a computer room, school garden, wheelchair-accessible features (parking, elevator, WC), and partnerships with local groups like Lernnetzwerk Akzent e.V., Gemeinwesenverein Haselhorst e.V., and Jugendkulturzentrum Haveleck.[5] Recent history notes addressing space shortages with temporary containers in 2016.[10] Community events highlight engagement, such as peace projects inspired by Udo Lindenberg and collaborations like "Spandau bewegt sich".[8]

### 3. CURRICURICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum follows Berlin's primary school standards with **Jahrgangsbezogenes Lernen (JabL)** starting from grade 3, English as the first foreign language, and **Schulanfangsphase** with mixed-age learning groups.[1][5] Special offerings include **offene Ganztagbetreuung (OGB)**, school social work (**Schulsozialarbeit**), and extracurricular activities (**Arbeitsgemeinschaften**) like theater, ceramics, cheerleading, and skating.[5] Emphasis on media education, conflict resolution, violence-free learning, and the "Tanzende Schule" program.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in year-round events like Waldlauf (forest runs), Fasching (carnival), Schülerparlament (student parliament), and mobility days, fostering community and physical activity.[8] After-school care exceeds basic supervision via **Hort Goldmühle**.[1][7] Extracurriculars and projects promote creativity, such as peace initiatives (**PULVERMÜHLE PEACE PROJECT**), Vorlesewettbewerb (reading competitions), and themed workshops on fake news vs. cyberbullying.[8] Daily structure includes defined **Unterrichtszeiten** (lesson times) and an **Auszeitraum** (break area).[9]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Achievements include successes in the Vorlesewettbewerb (reading competition), hosting the Alba Grundschulliga tournament, and a double jubilee for school and Hort.[8] The school is recognized as a "Tanzende Schule" and participates in district initiatives like "Spandau bewegt sich".[1][8] Enrollment stands at 677 students, with active community involvement in events like Christmas singing, Advent bazaars, and peace projects.[1][5][8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free Grundschule**, admissions follow Berlin's standard public school enrollment process based on residential district (Spandau, Haselhorst).[1][5] Contact via sekretariat@gs-pulvermuehle.de or 030-33776567 for inquiries; events like Tag der offenen Tür (open day) facilitate visits.[2][8] Leadership: Schulleiterin Anja Parizek.[1][2][6]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Grundschule an der Rennbahn emphasizes modern, flexible learning in a compartment-style (Compartmentschule) building designed to support diverse teaching forms, individual student needs, and collaborative environments through multifunctional spaces, workshops, and interactive technology.[4][6] It operates as a public Ganztagsschule (all-day school) in open form, fostering social learning, violence prevention, and holistic development via schulsozialarbeit partnerships.[1][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded for the 2022/23 school year in Berlin-Pankow (Weißensee), the school started with operations in a temporary location and moved into its new Compartmentbauweise building in February 2024, offering space for 570 students (planned capacity up to 720).[4][1][2][3] The modern campus includes interactive whiteboards in all classrooms, Lernwerkstätten (kitchen, natural sciences, music, art, wood), a multipurpose room with stage, library, and a two-story competition sports hall.[4][6] Leadership includes Schulleiterin Kathrin Gustke and Konrektorin Laura Pohrte; the team comprises 29 teachers and 15 educators for supplementary care (eFöB).[4] Community ties involve schulsozialarbeit by djo-Bildungswerk since February 2023, supporting families and social integration.[1] Plans include transitioning to a Gemeinschaftsschule by 2027/28.[4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school follows Berlin's Grundschule curriculum with English as the first foreign language, incorporating open-form all-day structure, compartment learning (Lernhäuser), and specialized workshops for hands-on subjects like arts, sciences, music, and crafts.[4][6][7] Current class distribution (2025/26): 7 entry-phase classes (grades 1-2), three grade 3s, two grade 4s, two grade 5s, and three Willkommensklassen (welcome classes for integration).[4] Extracurriculars include AG-Angebote (activity groups), midday meals, and eFöB programs with events like child discos and holiday plans.[4][6][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Approximately 360 students (recent figures vary: 200-360) learn in 17 classes, with modern facilities promoting interactive, flexible learning via whiteboards, workshops, outdoor "green classroom," and sports hall activities.[4][1][3] All-day offerings feature supervised afternoons, social-pedagogical support, violence prevention, and inclusive programs like Willkommensklassen; recent events include holiday programs and themed discos.[1][4][8] Enrollment grew post-move, with capacity for expansion.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific academic outcomes or test results are detailed in available content; the school is newly established (opened 2022) and focuses on infrastructure milestones like the February 2024 building move and official opening despite construction delays (e.g., water damage).[3][4] It recruits staff for growth, including sports teachers and eFöB roles, indicating ongoing development toward full capacity and Gemeinschaftsschule status.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule, admission follows Berlin's standard public school enrollment process via local authorities (Schulanmeldung); no specific fees or private application details are mentioned.[7] The school serves the Pankow-Weißensee area, with capacity for 570-720 students and current classes including integration-focused Willkommensklassen.[2][4] Contact sekretariat@03g49.schule.berlin.de or +49 30 92091798 for inquiries; sekretariat hours: Mon-Wed/Fri 7:30-14:00, Thu 7:30-15:00.[4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located directly along the Wuhle river in Berlin's Marzahn-Hellersdorf district (Hellersdorf neighborhood), the school serves 730 students in 30 classes plus a welcome class for newcomers, divided across three buildings.[1][2] Originally built in the 1990s as a DDR-type modular classroom structure (MUR) using large-panel construction, one building was renovated for modern learning forms, including pollutant removal, fire safety upgrades, and energy efficiency improvements.[3] It operates as an open all-day school (offene Ganztagsschule) with community ties via partnerships like ErzählZeit for language support, kindergartens, seniors programs, youth services, police, and sports clubs.[1][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum stresses differentiated instruction, reading competency development, and general education, with English as the first foreign language and offerings like year-group learning groups in early grades.[1][8] Extracurricular programs include free afternoon working groups (unentgeltliche Arbeitsgemeinschaften) such as basketball, martial arts, school gardening, sports, swimming, music/instrumental lessons, and fairy tale hours, scheduled on specific weekdays for grades 1-6.[1] Additional supports encompass specific supplementary classes, open all-day operations with homework help and holiday care for grades 1-4, and a welcome class.[1][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students benefit from a nature-oriented setting by the river, promoting personalized all-day experiences beyond routine "good" school days, with free library access, cultural/sports events, and self-directed activities in working groups.[1] The open all-day model offers leisure shaping, learning assistance, holiday supervision, and a calm, appreciative environment fostering traditions and extraordinary highlights.[1] Diverse offerings like instrumental music, gardening, and sports encourage free expression, alongside language programs like ErzählZeit for non-native speakers.[1][8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific achievements, test results, or outcomes are detailed in the provided content; general quality indicators like management professionalism are referenced but not quantified.[8] The school maintains partnerships for prevention, cultural education, and extracurriculars, with a renovated facility supporting modern learning.[3][8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
School enrollment and mandatory pre-enrollment medical exams by the child and youth health service (KJGD) are required for entry; first-grade enrollment celebration is set for September 13, 2025, with the 2025/2026 school year starting September 8, 2025, and a study day on October 2, 2025.[1] Contact details include phone (030 56 12 12 7), fax (030 56 29 79 18), and email (sekretariat@anderwuhle.schule.berlin.de).[1][8]

Located in Berlin-Tempelhof (Tempelhof-Schöneberg district, school number 07G21), the public primary school (Grundschule) serves 569 students in 24 classes, enrolling 100 new students annually in a four-track (vierzügig) structure with eight homogeneous groups in the Schulanfangsphase (SAPh: four 1st and four 2nd grade classes).[1][2][3][5] Facilities include six learning areas: fliegende Klassenzimmer (7 classrooms), Neubau (6), upper Westflügel/Lehrerzimmergang (4), full Ostflügel (7), Westflügel/Schulburg, plus canteen (Mensa), gym (Turnhalle), specialist rooms for sciences, music, art, and two computer rooms; a large green playground co-designed by children, covered path to renovated gym, and accessible features like parking for disabled and wheelchair ramps.[1][4][5][8] Staff comprises 42 teachers and 30 educators, plus three special educators, a school psychologist, heilpädagogen, secretary Deborah Niemann, janitor Peter Krause, and OGB cooperation with Nachbarschafts- und Selbsthilfezentrum (NUSZ).[1][5][7] Catering by Warnhoff offers certified (since 2012) balanced meals with organic products and daily vegetarian options.[1] Community ties include parent-led initiatives and OGB Schulburg (open all-day care) from 06:00-18:00 for up to 630 students.[1][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
2019 inspection highlights strengths in supportive school climate, reading/language promotion, varied instruction, barrier support, and collaborative pedagogy; students successfully transition post-primary.[1][4] Profiles as reading and theater-playing school with soccer team; parent engagement evident in events and newspaper.[1][5]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule im Blumenviertel is a state-run public primary school (Grundschule) with a **Montessori profile**, emphasizing discovery-based and self-directed learning supported by projects and free work periods.[1][3] The school promotes independence, quoting Maria Montessori: the aim is to "help the child become independent" through individualized learning that respects each child's development.[2] It fosters an inclusive environment where children with and without special needs learn together, prioritizing self-responsibility and community.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Built in the 1970s as a Plattenbau (prefabricated concrete building), the school underwent major renovations in 2004-2005, including new windows, roof, interiors, and facade.[1] Recent upgrades include a new sports field with modern surfacing for safety and play, plus a three-field sports hall opened recently with large windows, barrier-free access, stacked changing rooms, and solar panels for sustainability; the hall serves students and local clubs.[1] Located in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg (Pankow district), it operates as an open all-day school (offene Ganztagsgrundschule) with strong community ties, including events like Christmas singing and holiday programs.[6][8] Leadership: Anja Liebs (current); previously Fr. Helbig.[6][8] A supporting association promotes Montessori pedagogy.[9]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum follows **Montessori pedagogy** with year-spanning learning groups: grades 1-3 together and 4-6 together, enabling differentiated, free-form instruction without grades until grade 5.[1][2][8] English is taught as the first foreign language; the school is three-track (3-zügig) with about 360 students, 273 using supplementary all-day care in classrooms for grades 1-3.[2][6][8] Focus areas include individual learning prerequisites, projects, and inclusive support for special needs.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in self-directed, project-based learning in mixed-age groups, promoting autonomy, teamwork, and hands-on exploration.[1][2][3] All-day care integrates into open learning spaces; recent facilities enhance physical activity with renovated playgrounds, sports fields, and a modern hall for PE and clubs.[1] Community events like costume parties during winter holidays (e.g., Feb 2-6, 2026) and Christmas singing build social bonds.[Main Page] Inklusion is core, with teams of teachers and educators addressing diverse needs, though staffing was under-resourced at ~90% in past inspections.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Inspections highlight strengths in Montessori-based independent learning and differentiated offers in mixed-age groups.[2] The school successfully integrates inklusion, with positive community feedback on new sports facilities improving daily movement and safety.[1] It participates in programs like SPI (since 08.2021) via LHS Lebenshilfe and cultural projects funded in 2009 (e.g., Hip Hop for respect).[7][9] No specific test scores or outcomes detailed, but the model supports self-responsible growth into adulthood per Montessori principles.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school** owned by Bezirksamt Pankow, admission follows Berlin's standard public primary school process via Schulanmeldung.[Ownership][8] Open to all local children; inklusive for those with special needs.[1][2] Contact: Syringenplatz 30, 10407 Berlin; Tel +49 30 42850870; email 03G38@03G38.schule.berlin.de.[6][8] Events like open doors listed in Berlin calendars.[8]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule im Eliashof positions itself as a neighborhood (**Kiezschule**) school emphasizing community collaboration among leadership, teachers, educators, parents, and students to rebuild and shape the school collectively.[9] It promotes living diversity together (**Gemeinsam Vielfalt leben**) and offers a reliable half-day program with cross-grade learning, open all-day care (**Offene Ganztagbetreuung**), and a pedagogical approach integrating social-pedagogical support via tjfbg gGmbH since 2012/13.[1][2][5][9]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established as a two-track primary school since the 2010/11 school year in the Eliashof area near the Berlin Wall, it has grown to over 350 students from grades 1-6 by 2019/20.[4][9] Located in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg (Pankow district), it fosters strong local ties through partnerships with neighboring kindergartens (**Kitas** like Kritzelknirpse and Eliaskita), Béla Bartók Music School, Sportverein Empor Berlin, MachMit!Museum, and Buchhandlung Buchbox.[9] Leadership includes Armin Geißler (current head) and social-pedagogical head Herr Segeth (tjfbg gGmbH); a diverse team of teachers, educators, and staff brings varied qualifications.[5][8][9] Contact: Senefelderstr. 6, 10437 Berlin; Tel: 030 40504800; Email: sekretariat@03g46.schule.berlin.de.[1][3][5][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves grades 1-6 as a public primary school (**Grundschule**) with English as the first foreign language, cross-grade and year-group learning, and elective **Humanistische Lebenskunde** (secular ethics based on scientific insights into nature and society, following a 2023 framework plan).[1][5][6] Lebenskunde covers three thematic areas—personal development in social contexts, responsibility for nature and society (including democracy and digitalization), and world orientation/sense-making—taught without grades by Herr Zenker (T.Zenker@hvd-bb.de); open to all students under 14 with parental consent.[6] Includes school social work (**Schulsozialarbeit**) and afternoon care by tjfbg gGmbH.[2][9]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience a half-day structure with collaborative projects emphasizing neighborhood integration, music, sports, and intercultural learning; daily routines include lessons, breaks, and optional all-day care.[5][9] Elective Lebenskunde encourages reflection on personal experiences, life's meaning, and non-religious humanist views alongside knowledge of religions and philosophies, adapted to age levels with student input.[6] School rules, parent involvement via Förderverein, and social-pedagogical support enhance daily life.[3][9]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific achievements, test results, or outcomes detailed in available content; an inspection report exists but lacks accessible details.[10] Growth from ~275 students (2010/11) to over 350 (2019/20) indicates stable community embedding.[4][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free school owned by Bezirksamt Pankow, admission follows Berlin's standard primary school enrollment for local residents; open all-day care and electives like Lebenskunde available with parental opt-in for those under 14.[1][5] Contact sekretariat for details; school number 03G46.[2][5][7]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule im Hofgarten operates as a **public primary school** (Grundschule) emphasizing an inclusive, garden-like environment described as "Ein Garten für alle" (A Garden for All), fostering a supportive space amid urban surroundings.[10][3] It follows a **Ganztagsschule in offener Form** model with open-form all-day schooling, year-group-based learning groups in the early phase, and a focus on student autonomy, creativity, and community through elements like monthly rules, a school garden for projects, and collaborative team teaching by teachers and educators.[1][7][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The school building, constructed in 1893/94 under city building councilor Hermann Blankenstein as a boys' community school, has been continuously used for education and was repurposed as a primary school due to rising enrollment in Prenzlauer Berg.[3] Located at Danziger Str. 50, 10435 Berlin (Prenzlauer Berg), it serves over **400 students** with around **45 staff members**, including teachers, educators, special needs support, school assistants, two school dogs, and university trainees.[9][7][5] Facilities include a quiet inner courtyard oasis with a "Hextendor" entrance, north garden (with school garden for lessons and EFöB), south courtyard, asphalt path, 220-person aula, creative workshop, wood workshop, playroom, library, club room, and homework rooms.[3][4][6] The district is Pankow, owned publicly by Bezirksamt Pankow von Berlin (school number 03G45), with leadership by Desiree Englbrecht (acting principal) and administrative support.[1][5][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum features **English as a foreign language**, profile courses starting in 5th grade extending to 6th, and open-form structures with magnetic overview boards for student-chosen interests in learning, relaxation, research, movement, and play.[1][4] All-day programs (Ganztag) include external offerings (EFöB) like over 40 AGs (working groups) such as Keramikschule (ceramics with professional artists), Orchesterschule (instrumental lessons, ensembles, free trials by certified pedagogues), and Tim Findig (hands-on natural science fostering curiosity for ages 4-10).[6] Additional elements cover morning/abschluss circles, class duties, author visits on World Book Day, and partnerships for music and projects.[4][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in vibrant activities like **podcast "Streberecke"** (student-led episodes on topics like animals and holidays), reading competitions, magical themed events with costumes/games, class trips (e.g., to Schloss Boitzenburg), Easter crafts, Christmas decorations, and all-school events such as open doors and festivals.[10] Emphasis on **inclusion, student participation, mediation, competitions, annual activities, and profile courses** promotes holistic development, with spaces for play, homework, and creativity; meals and after-school care are available.[website content][4][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Specific outcomes are not detailed, but the school highlights ongoing student successes in **reading competitions** (e.g., 6th graders in 2025), podcasts (4+ seasons), cultural events, and collaborations like school orchestras performing at festivals and pauses.[website content][6] It maintains a dynamic community with 27+ news posts, parent involvement via Förderverein (donations, projects, school shirts), and steady enrollment growth.[website content][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, admission follows Berlin's standard process; prospective families are invited to **Tag der Offenen Tür** (Open Day) on September 30, 2025, from 10:30-11:45 for first-graders and parents.[website content] Additional resources cover school enrollment (Schulanmeldung), school route (Schulweg), and FAQs.[website content]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto, **"GEMEINSAM LERNEN UND LEBEN"** (Together Learn and Live), emphasizes collaborative learning and community living.[1][4] It participates in the "Empathie macht Schule" model project, promoting empathy to enhance learning, as children learn better in supportive environments.[1][4] The philosophy focuses on a **sportbetontes Profil** (sports-oriented profile) in cooperation with professional club Füchse Berlin, diverse teaching methods, modern media, and open all-day care from 6:00-18:00 for holistic development.[1][2][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established with a 50th anniversary in 2025, the school was reconstructed in 1996.[1][7] Located in a quiet area on the edge of Weißensee (Berlin-Pankow district), it serves as a **dreizügige Grundschule** (three-track primary school) with public ownership by Bezirksamt Pankow.[1][2][6] Facilities include a spacious outdoor area with **Schulgarten** (school garden), renovated gym for events, art and creativity room, science lab, student kitchen, computer room, interactive whiteboards, and mobile tablets; digitalization advanced since 2020.[1] Community involvement features parent and student participation in committees, partnerships like Tanzwerkstatt NoLimit and Seniorpartners, and a supportive school climate praised in inspections.[1][4][6] Leadership: Fr. Schön (Franziska Schön).[2][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Ganztagsschule in offener Form** (open all-day school), it offers **English** as the first foreign language, sports emphasis, health-focused profile, language profile, environmental education, and reading/writing support groups.[2][6] Grades 5-6 provide weekly elective courses; afternoons include working groups (AGs) in sports, woodworking, PC, ceramics, choir, clowns, and trick films.[1][4][6] Teaching integrates modern media, individualized learning, and year-group learning clusters in early phases.[1][6] Sports programs include annual Handballaktionstag and cross-country runs.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in afternoon choices of musical, artistic, and sports activities, with non-AG participants selecting alternatives.[1][4] Extracurriculars feature **Schulband "Moselgang"** for grades 3-6, school library, and garden projects.[1][4] Events enrich daily life: enrollment ceremonies, open days, harvest festivals, sponsor runs, parent-child days, reading days, Christmas musicals, Halloween discos, Poland trips, and "Zu Fuß zur Schule" mobility actions.[1][4] Blog highlights include Olympian training, apple pressing, and forest outings.[1] Parental events like Eltern-Kind-Turnen promote movement play.[3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
School inspections (every 5 years since 2005) in April 2025 praised the **wertschätzende und positive Atmosphäre** (appreciative and positive atmosphere) and interpersonal relations; 2014 review also positive.[7] Sports successes include handball awards and league participation.[1][4] Recent blog notes victories like handball siegerehrung.[1] Positive inspection feedback on teaching processes per Berlin's Schulqualität framework.[7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Grundschule (öffentlich)**, admission follows Berlin's standard primary school enrollment by residence in the catchment area (Weißensee, with filial at Sulzfelder Str. 15).[1][6] Events like Einschulungsfeier (enrollment ceremony), Tag der offenen Tür (open day), and Eltern-Kind-Tag support transitions; contact sekretariat@mosel.schule.berlin.de or 030-9250976 for details.[1][4][5]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Grundschule im Panketal is a **sport-oriented and disability-friendly primary school** (LRS-Standortschule) located in Berlin-Karow, welcoming students, parents, and visitors to explore its offerings.[6] It operates as a full-day school in open form (Ganztagsschule in offener Form) with cross-grade learning groups in the early school phase, emphasizing English as a foreign language, information technology as a profiled subject, and elective activities (AG-Angebot).[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established on September 4, 1995, as the 9th Grundschule in the Karow-Nord new development area, the school was ceremonially opened after a 14-month construction period.[3][1] In October 1998, it received the name "Grundschule im Panketal" following a student project week on local history.[7] The school has celebrated milestones like its 10th birthday in 2005 and 15th in 2010 with events such as a courtyard festival.[8][9] Facilities include an auditorium, modern language lab, student library, large gym with retractable bleachers, modeling room, two computer rooms, and wheelchair-accessible cafeteria equipment.[4][1][2] It is publicly owned by Bezirksamt Pankow von Berlin, led by Hr. Mannsfeld, and serves the community at Achillesstr. 31, 13125 Berlin (contact: 030 94794920, gs-im-panketal@gmx.de).[2][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum features **English as a foreign language**, **information technology** as a profiled area, cross-grade learning groups in the Schulanfangsphase, and elective offerings (AG-Angebot).[2] The school supports open-form full-day education with specialized facilities like computer rooms and a language lab.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students benefit from a sport-focused environment with a large gym, disability-friendly design including wheelchair-accessible facilities, and hands-on spaces like the library, modeling room, and language lab.[1][2][4][6] Full-day structure includes midday meals with rollstuhlgerechte Ausstattung.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific achievements or outcomes are detailed in available sources; the profile highlights longevity (over 30 years since 1995) and community events marking anniversaries.[3][7][8][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule, admissions follow standard Berlin public school procedures based on local residency; no specific process details are provided in sources.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The **Grundschule im Taunusviertel** was built in 1998 at Wiesbadener Str. 20, 12309 Berlin (Lichtenrade), with the foundation stone laid in late September or early October that year. The school was completed in summer 1999 and officially opened for the 1999/2000 school year, marking its 20th anniversary around 2019[1]. The campus spans approximately 9,000 square meters and includes about 45 rooms, with 13 currently used as classrooms. Specialized rooms cover evangelical religion, life skills (Lebenskunde), music, art, and natural sciences; a first-floor computer room has 15 workstations; and smaller spaces support part-time classes, English, and German as a second language (DaZ) instruction. The basement features material storage and a multi-purpose room with a stage for events like enrollment ceremonies and Christmas performances. Administrative areas include a teachers' lounge, secretariat, and two principal offices. Outdoor facilities comprise a large divisible sports hall, extensive schoolyard with grass areas, playground, table tennis tables, climbing frame, sports field, and a games lending system during breaks; Catholic religious education occurs in a separate caretaker's house[1]. The school serves the Lichtenrade community as a public primary school (Grundschule)[1][2].

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school emphasizes a welcoming atmosphere without early specialization, fostering a "We-consciousness" through rules and rituals, while promoting values like respect, tolerance, non-violent conflict resolution, helpfulness, environmental and health awareness, teamwork, discipline, and democratic understanding.[7] It prioritizes trust-based collaboration among parents, after-school care (Hort), and school in education and upbringing tasks, alongside developing learning techniques via diverse methods, individual personalities, creativity, responsibility, and curiosity.[7] The school opens to the local community (Kiez) with diverse events beyond regular offerings.[7][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Reinickendorf (Waidmannslust area), the public primary school serves the local neighborhood with partnerships including Horizonte gGmbH for youth social work, school social workers, and educational coordinators.[1][2][5] Leadership includes Rektor Alexander Franz (since at least 2022, signed updates in May 2024) and Konrektorin Sabine Baumann-Kühn, with a multiprofessional team of teachers, educators, social workers, administration, and coordinators.[7][8][6] Recent developments focus on school improvement, including better resource use and learning conditions; facilities support open classroom doors and hallway learning.[1][7] Contact: Tel. 030-4023059, Fax 030-4029742, email sekretariat@rollberge.schule.berlin.de.[3][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The curriculum features year-crossing learning groups (1-3 and adaptations 4-6 via JüL-System), class teams (two classes collaborating on projects since 22/23, with first-graders in small German groups since 23/24), and plans for an additional A-class integration.[7][5] First foreign language is English; offerings include open all-day care (OGB), introductory phase with cross-grade groups, school station (Schulstation), and extracurriculars like crafting, singing/dancing, computer (grades 1/2), soccer, handicrafts, judo, kitchen/baking, school garden, play/fun, pottery, experiments, theater.[5] Methods emphasize project and team work inside/outside school.[7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience open classrooms with hallway use for learning, intensive teacher-student collaboration in class teams for joint projects, and focus on strengths, curiosity, and social competencies.[7][1] Daily life includes multiprofessional support for improved learning situations, with an emphasis on personality development, creativity, and responsibility.[7] Bildergalerie showcases current school life; upcoming Open Day on September 26, 2025, invites future first-graders' parents for insights into teaching.[7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
School development advances include JüL-System review leading to class teams (22/23), team adjustments (23/24), and enhanced multiprofessional collaboration.[7] Ongoing goals: build additional A-class, optimize spaces, and sustain team improvements (noted May 2024).[7] No specific exam results or quantitative outcomes detailed; participates in programs like SPI since October 2022 via Horizonte gGmbH.[2][5]

Public primary school (tuition-free) with Open Day on September 26, 2025, especially for future first-graders' parents to observe open classrooms and pedagogy.[7][5] No specific admission processes detailed beyond standard Berlin public enrollment; contact sekretariat for inquiries.[3]

The school emphasizes a child-centered approach, integrating themes from students' everyday lives with diverse teaching methods, differentiation, and promotion at all levels to foster curiosity, openness to new experiences, lifelong learning, and joy in life.[1] It operates as an inclusive full-day school (Ganztagsschule), actively shaped in partnership with OPTIMUS Bildungspartner to provide interest- and strengths-oriented offerings tailored to each child's uniqueness.[1][2][10] Key elements include Frühenglisch (early English from first grade, one hour weekly, focusing playfully on vocabulary and pronunciation) and a commitment to cooperation, method variety, and external learning sites.[1][9]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in summer 2018 with two first-grade classes, the school has expanded rapidly: by 2022/23 it had ten classes (grades 1-5), reaching full status as a **zweizügige Grundschule** (two-track primary school, grades 1-6) for the 2023/24 school year, serving approximately **300 students**.[1][5] Located in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (Plantagenstr. 8-9, 12169 Berlin), the building dates to 1888 and supports around 242 students with 21 teachers as of recent data.[1][3] It partners with OPTIMUS for full-day care (open Ganztag, capacity 210 places, contact: plantagen@diebildungspartner.de, Ganztagsleitung: Markus Schiffer), offering free midday meals.[1][2][6] The community includes active events like Christmas markets, reading competitions, lantern singing, Halloween, and collaborations such as with Flying Steps Academy for music/dance.[scraped content]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a public German **Grundschule** (primary school, grades 1-6), the curriculum follows Berlin state standards with first foreign language English (Frühenglisch from grade 1).[1][3] Programs include open school start (Offener Schulbeginn), extracurricular activities (Arbeitsgemeinschaften), school social work (Schulsozialarbeit), promotion and challenge concepts (Fördern und Fordern), and full-day offerings with concepts like shared educational understanding (Gemeinsames Bildungsverständnis) and learning/support plans (Lern- und Förderkonzept).[scraped content][9] Special initiatives cover eFöB (likely digital education), WPU courses (e.g., "Musik um uns" with dance), and inclusive full-day structuring.[1][2][10]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in a vibrant, inclusive environment with full-day options from early morning (6:00) to evening (18:00), featuring playful language learning, music/dance collaborations, reading contests, seasonal events (e.g., lantern singing, Halloween, Christmas bazaar), and outdoor activities in the school garden.[1][2][scraped content] The school promotes method diversity, individual support, and real-world themes via external sites, with free meals enhancing daily life; class mascots (e.g., Capybaras, Pandas) add community spirit.[1][scraped content]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific academic outcomes or test results are detailed in available content, though the school highlights successful events like the 2025/2026 reading competition (with grade 6 participants), first Christmas bazaar, and cooperative programs indicating strong community engagement.[scraped content] Growth to full two-track status with ~300 students reflects successful expansion.[1][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Grundschule in Rosenthal emphasizes **reading and violence prevention** as core focuses, aiming to foster joy in learning through innovative knowledge transmission in line with the Berliner Bildungsprogramm.[2] It promotes responsible media use, digital preparedness with interactive whiteboards and hardware, and blends traditional and modern teaching methods while prioritizing safety and data protection via a **Computer-Nutzungsordnung**.[2] The school supports dedicated educators in creating unique learning environments and collaborates with parents for child-centered education.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Rosenthal (Pankow district), this **public primary school (Grundschule, school number 03G13)** operates from a main building and modular extension, offering facilities like computer labs, a school library, creative design workshop, relaxation room, and full internet access.[1][2] Community involvement is strong through the **Förderverein** (parent support association) for tax-deductible sponsorships, **Elternvertretung** (parent council led by Sophia Paul, Michael Vormeier, and others), and events like Lantern Walks and "Kehrenbürger" clean-up actions.[5] After-school care (Hort) is managed by partner **tjfbg gGmbH** since 08.2021, with a team including coordinators like Frau Müller and educators across classes.[3][6] Leadership: Schulleiter Hendrik Nitsch; Verwaltungsleiterin Frau Runge.[2] Contact: +49 30 91699480, sekretariat@rosenthal.schule.berlin.de; secretariat hours Mon-Tue 7:30-10:30 & 11:30-14:00, Wed-Fri adjusted.[4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **reliable half-day school with full-day options**, it provides open all-day care (OGB) from 6:00-7:30 and post-school until 18:00, including year-group and cross-grade learning in early phases.[1][2] Languages: English or French as first foreign language.[1] Extracurriculars (AGs) cover sports, cooking, creative arts, digital media, choir, and external partnerships; social-pedagogical support aids daily school life.[1][6] Digital integration prepares for future worlds, with emphasis on reading promotion via monthly favorite books and reading nights.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in vibrant activities like Harry Potter-themed reading nights, Christmas library events, Lantern Walks, and puzzles.[main] Sports include Sparki-Cup football (girls' team 5th place, boys' strong participation), Streetball Challenge, and Deutscher Motoriktest for movement promotion.[5][main] Reading competitions yield winners (e.g., Kolja in 4th grade), Big Challenge English contest, and projects like "Lieblingsbücher des Monats." Hort AGs offer diverse options for every interest; external providers expand offerings via direct contracts.[main][6] Parental resources address school challenges via Pankow programs.[main]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Notable successes: Girls' team 5th at Sparki-Cup 2026; strong showings in Streetball Challenge (team "Rosen im Tal"); positive Deutscher Motoriktest results with talent identification; reading competition winners across grades 4-6; participation in "Big Challenge" English.[main][5] Events like magical reading nights and Ferienreisen highlight engagement; violence prevention and reading foci support holistic development.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public school with **tuition-free** access; enrollment period for new students announced annually (e.g., 2024 notices).[2][7] Parents receive pre-enrollment letters from leadership; apply via standard Berlin processes. After-school Hort requires need-based applications with forms via tjfbg partner.[main][3] Info events for transitions (e.g., to grade 7 on 29.01.2026).[main]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule Luisenstadt operates on three core pillars: **gemeinsam leben** (living together), **individuell wachsen** (individual growth), and **kollaborativ lernen** (collaborative learning).[1] The school fosters respectful interactions in stable home groups that value diversity, aiming to develop confident, creative, and responsible societal members beyond mere knowledge transmission.[1] Collaborative learning prepares students for a networked world through teamwork and social skills, supported by a multiprofessional team, while individual growth addresses varied developmental stages with personalized training in reading, writing, and math during dedicated learning time.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The school is a newly built public primary school (Grundschule) in Berlin-Mitte's Luisenstadt district, replacing an unused prefab ruin at Adalbertstraße 53, 10179 Berlin, to meet rising demand for school places.[2][3][5] Constructed as part of Berlin's Schulbauoffensive II for €64 million in modular Compartmentbauweise (compartment design with central forum for flexible learning), it opened for the 2024/25 school year on September 12, 2024, with capacity for **576 students** across four classes (vierzügig).[2][3][5] Facilities include a new stacked double sports hall, modern outdoor areas (completed spring 2025), and art integration like Stef Heidhues' "Wo die wilden Tiere wohnen."[3][4] The community supports via GEV (parents' council) and Förderverein for projects and funding; school leader is Frau Rother, with key staff including Konrektorin Samel, leitende Erzieherin Driesener, and sekretariat Segelken.[1][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Compartmentschule**, the school emphasizes all-day programs (Ganztag) with learning ateliers, projects, and partnerships like ALBA BERLIN.[1][8] It offers first foreign languages **English** and **French**, focuses on culture and movement, and follows Berlin's standards for new school builds.[1][6][7] Core academics prioritize basic competencies in reading, writing, and arithmetic via individualized Lernzeit, integrated with collaborative and social elements.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience stable Stammgruppen for social support, collaborative projects preparing for teamwork, and personalized potential development in a modern, flexible compartment layout.[1][2] Activities include events like Lichterfest, ADAC Roller Fit, Max Artists in Residence art projects, and neighborhood shaping initiatives; daily routines feature play, research, discovery, and all-day care with holiday openings.[1][8] Emphasis on well-being in multiprofessional settings, with AGs (interest groups), Hort (after-school care), and inclusive environments.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific academic outcomes or test results are detailed in available content, as the school is newly opened (2024/25 school year).[1][2][5] Achievements include successful construction under Schulbauoffensive, architectural award to Bruno Fioretti Marquez (2018), and community projects like art residencies and traffic safety programs.[1][3][4][5] Upcoming events: enrollment celebration September 13, 2025.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free Grundschule with open enrollment via Berlin's Schulanmeldung process; first school day for 2024/25 was September 2, 2024, with enrollment September 7, 2024.[6][8] Tag der offenen Tür (open day) planned for 2025/26 intake; contact sekretariat@01G49.schule.berlin.de or +49 172 8339016 for inquiries, krankmeldungen by 8:00 AM.[1][6] EFöB (early support) via Stefanie Driesener.[1]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule Neues Tor is a public primary school emphasizing **interculturalism**, **bilingualism** in German and Portuguese, and lifelong learning competencies through individualized, project-oriented education.[1][3][5] It promotes democratic education, open communication, transparency, diverse expression (linguistic, scientific, artistic, playful), healthy nutrition via a school garden and kitchen, sports, and rejection of racism and discrimination, fostering European openness and cultural diversity symbolized by "swimming sardines."[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established with a Deutsch-Portugiesisch SESB (Staatliche Europa-Schule Berlin) branch since **1998**, the school building dates to 1888-1889, designed by Hermann Blankenstein as the 146th and 180th community school; formerly known as the 10th Grundschule Berlin-Mitte in 1955.[1][5][6] Located in **Berlin-Mitte (Alt-Mitte)** near the Charité at Hannoversche Str. 20, 10115 Berlin, it serves a diverse community with bilingual German-Portuguese families and local residents; it partners with tjfbg gGmbH since 2008/09 for all-day operations.[1][2][8] Facilities include a sports hall (recently mural-painted), school garden, kitchen, and open/gebundener Ganztag (all-day school).[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school operates as a **zweizügige (two-stream) SESB Deutsch-Portugiesisch** for bilingual immersion (50% German, 50% Portuguese instruction in small groups of ~12 by native speakers) and an **einzügige (one-stream) Regelschule** for local catchment children.[1][4][5] Core elements include year-group mixed learning (grades 1-3), homogeneous classes (grade 4+), English as first foreign language, Wahlpflichtkurse in grades 5/6 ("Soziales Lernen" and "Einblicke in die portugiesischsprachige Welt"), Evangelical Religion or Humanistische Lebenskunde; SESB emphasizes integrated bilingual education.[2][4][7] The 2017 Schulprogramm focuses on communication culture, school-wide development, learner-oriented instruction, and structured leadership.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience **gebundener Ganztag** (bound all-day) in SESB for ~250 children and **offener Ganztag** (open all-day) in Regelschule for ~107, with ~9 children in integrative special needs support; total staff includes 25 German-speaking and 15 Portuguese-speaking teachers.[1][8][10] Activities promote intercultural projects, festivals, sports (e.g., Mini-Marathon), arts (sardine designs, murals), garden-based nutrition, Zeitzeugen visits, and community events like donations to Berliner Stadtmission.[1][3][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recent highlights include a successful 5th annual donation drive (116 gift packages, Dec 2025), safe school route planning, alumni visits from 1955/56, new parents' council (2025/26), sports hall mural transformation, and Mini-Marathon participation (2024).[1] A 2015 inspection noted significant development needs in school program, learning conditions, and cooperation, recommending supervisory support.[8] No recent Prüfungsergebnisse or outcome metrics provided.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public **tuition-free** Grundschule, admission prioritizes SESB for children seeking German-Portuguese bilingualism and Regelschule for local Einzugsbereich residents welcoming the profile; contact via Tel: +49 30 24088330, Email: 01G05@01G05.schule.berlin.de, Leitung: Diana Mihov.[1][2] Transition to Oberschule supported by informational brochure on forms, all-day offers, and languages.[9]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule unter dem Regenbogen emphasizes the development of skills, knowledge, and abilities for all children, preparing them for life's challenges while supporting personal and professional growth. The school prioritizes respectful and friendly interactions within its community, placing each child's uniqueness at the center of its pedagogical efforts, and operates as an inclusive **Ganztagsschule in offener Form** (all-day school in open form) fostering diverse interests and abilities under one roof.[Main Page][1][3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Marzahn-Hellersdorf (Ortsteil Marzahn), near Springpfuhl park and Helene-Weigel-Platz, the school serves a diverse community including children with special needs in learning, language, emotional-social development, physical-motor skills, autism, intellectual development, and hearing. Facilities include two school buildings with a connecting tract, two gymnasiums, two schoolyards (Nordhof for grades 5-6, Südhof for lower grades), a school garden with a greenhouse built in November 2022 for sustainable education (BNE), and accessibility features like parking for disabled persons, wheelchair elevators, and restrooms. Community partnerships include Kids & Co for after-school care (7:30-13:30 school hours, extended by educators), JAO gGmbH for school social work since August 2020, pad gGmbH's T(Anker)-project, and participation in the INKA inclusion model trial since 2010/2011. A new branch with 22 classrooms is planned at Marzahner Chaussee 165.[School Information][Main Page][About Page][1][2][3][4][5][6]

### 3. CURRICURICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **5.5-zügige Grundschule** (5.5-stream primary school, Schulnummer 10G14), it offers inclusive education with year-group and cross-grade learning groups in the early phase, English as the first foreign language, theater-focused profile, and special support groups like T(Anker), "begleiteter Übergang ab Klasse 3," and for intellectual development needs district-wide. Programs include **offene Ganztagsbetreuung (OGB)**, school social work focusing on violence prevention, social learning, conflict resolution, and parental involvement, plus sustainability education via the school garden.[Main Page][About Page][1][3][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in hands-on activities like the annual Feuerprojekt (fire safety for grades 3-4 hort children, including volcano-building and cooking), Stromkasten-Styling art with Helliwood-Lernwerkstatt and police talks on graffiti, excursions (fire station, Atze Musiktheater, bowling), Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) such as schulgarten projects, sports (Bundesjugendspiele, Staffellauf), cultural events (Märchenaufführung "Frau Holle," Hoffest, Einschulung), and social offers in individual/group settings. Hortkinder are supervised between AGs; hauskinder require parental consent to go home independently post-AG.[Main Page][About Page][1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school has grown in student numbers and staff over the past five years, now using the former Barlach-Schule building, with ~300 children in open all-day care. Strengths include high staff engagement, a learning-friendly climate, and strong district/free-provider cooperation. It participates in inclusion models and offers theater, lernwerkstatt, and accessibility.[Main Page][1][3][4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free Grundschule**, admissions follow Berlin's standard public school enrollment process (Schulanmeldung). Contact Sekretariat at 030/54 98 98 80 or sekretariat@10g14.schule.berlin.de for details; events like Einschulung (e.g., 13 September) welcome new students.[School Information][Main Page][4]

# Grundschule unter den Bäumen - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **Grundschule unter den Bäumen** is built on a philosophy of "Lernen und Wohlfühlen" (Learning and Well-being) in a community-centered environment.[2] The school emphasizes that **support and learning go hand in hand**, with a focus on personal development and social competencies.[2] The educational approach centers on students experiencing not only the joy of learning but also the pleasure of discovering, exploring, and creating together.[2] The school's vision is to create an inclusive space where every child feels comfortable and is optimally supported.[2]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Opening**

The Grundschule unter den Bäumen is located at Alt-Blankenburg 26, 13127 Berlin, in the Blankenburg district (Ortsteil) of Pankow (Schulplanungsregion Nördliches Weißensee).[1][3] The school was officially opened as a modern new building offering capacity for 432 children and was realized as part of the Berlin School Construction Initiative (Berliner Schulbauoffensive).[1] It represents the fourth and final new construction in the second phase of this initiative.[1]

**Architectural Design and Facilities**

The three-story building was constructed using the Berlin Learning and Team House model.[1] Each floor contains two "Compartments" that together form a learning house, combining classrooms, differentiation areas, team zones, and a forum as the pedagogical center.[1] This structure promotes modern, inclusive, and all-day learning.[1] The school includes specialized rooms, learning workshops, a cafeteria with kitchen, a multipurpose room, and recreational areas.[1]

The campus features a **three-part sports hall** that serves both school and community sports, fully accessible and equipped with modern facilities.[1] Additional outdoor amenities include a sports field, running track, and school garden.[1] The valuable tree population on the site has been preserved as much as possible and supplemented with new plantings, creating a green campus that reflects the school's name and educational philosophy.[4]

**Current Enrollment**

The school currently serves approximately **469 students** organized in 18 classes, with a capacity of 5 class streams (5-zügig).[3][8] The school is staffed by 27 teachers and 16 additional educators.[8]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

The school offers instruction across a comprehensive range of subjects:[2]

- Deutsch (German)
- Englisch (English)
- GeWi (Social Studies)
- Kunst (Art)
- Mathematik (Mathematics)
- Musik (Music)
- NaWi (Natural Sciences)
- Religion & Lebenskunde (Religion & Life Skills)
- Sachunterricht (General Studies)
- Sport (Physical Education)

The curriculum is presented through an imaginative pedagogical framework that positions learning as an adventure of discovery, where students use various tools and collaborate as a team to explore different domains of knowledge.[2]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**All-Day Learning and Inclusive Education**

The school is designed to provide ideal conditions for **inclusive education and all-day programs**.[6] The Compartment model facilitates collaborative learning environments that support students with diverse learning needs.[6]

**School Life and Community Events**

The school maintains an active calendar of community events and celebrations, including:[2]

- Weihnachtsmarkt und -singen (Christmas Market and Caroling) - featuring festive music, lights, and community celebration
- Weißenseer Vorlesewettbewerb (Weißensee Reading Competition)
- Schul-Fasching (School Carnival)

**Feedback and Communication**

The school conducts regular feedback conversations where students, parents, and teachers openly and respectfully exchange information about learning progress, strengths, and goals.[2]

**School Meals**

All primary school students receive **free lunch daily** as mandated by Berlin law (since April 9, 2019).[2] Meals are freshly prepared and nutritionally balanced by Drei Köche GmbH, a catering company specializing in school meals for primary schools.[2] Parents register children online for meals, with ordering available until 6 PM the previous day.[2]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The school represents a significant achievement in Berlin's educational infrastructure as part of the School Construction Initiative. The building design has received recognition for its innovative approach to modern pedagogy, with the modular construction model (Compartment-Grundschule) winning a 2018 competition and serving as a template for 10 school locations across Berlin.[6] The first school using this model opened in September 2023 in Prenzlauer Berg, demonstrating the success of the design approach.[6]

The school's commitment to environmental stewardship and sustainability is reflected in its preservation of the existing tree population and integration of green spaces throughout the campus.[4]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

The search results provided do not contain specific information about admissions procedures, enrollment deadlines, or application requirements. Parents seeking admission information should contact the school directly through its website at https://www.gudbaeumen.de or visit the school office at Alt-Blankenburg 26, 13127 Berlin.

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school aims to create a supportive learning and living environment where children develop according to their individual competencies and feel secure, as stated in its mission and affirmed by the 2018/2019 inspection report.[3][4][9] It emphasizes a sport-focused profile with additional sports hours for grades 1-3 and afternoon sports electives (AG) for grades 4-6, alongside modern IT integration as an "eEducation Berlin Masterplan" school using interactive displays in all classrooms.[3][4][8]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1895 with an historic brick building (Altbau), the school was expanded with a new building in 2000, resulting in a well-maintained, bright facility in a residential area of Lichterfelde near S-Bahn Lichterfelde Ost, accessible by car, bike, bus, tram, and train.[1][3][4][9] The spacious schoolyard includes a soccer field, basketball court, climbing equipment, table tennis, long jump pit, and 75-meter running track; facilities also feature two sports halls, a multipurpose room with stage and theater tech connected to a garden with grill area, computer rooms, and barrier-free access via elevator and accessible toilets.[3][4][8] It serves the Steglitz-Zehlendorf district as a public, three-track (3-zügige) primary school (Grundschule) with open all-day care (offene Ganztagsbetreuung).[2][4][8]

### 3. CURRICUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a standard Berlin Grundschule (primary school for grades 1-6, school number 06G24), the curriculum includes English as the first foreign language, year-group learning groups in the early phase (Schulanfangsphase), and a sports emphasis with extra hours and internal/external competitions.[4][8][9] IT education is advanced with interactive 85-inch displays replacing smartboards, making classrooms "chalk-free," and ongoing equipment upgrades.[4] Open all-day program (OGB) supports after-school care in the new building.[4][8][9]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students benefit from diverse sports facilities and activities, including two sports halls for movement and play, participation in external competitions, and internal tournaments.[3][4] The open all-day structure with 13 educators enables early morning, afternoon, late, and full-day care; modern classrooms with whiteboards/displays and computer rooms enhance interactive learning in a secure, spacious environment.[3][4][8][9]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The 2018/2019 inspection report praises the school for largely meeting its mission of fostering individual development in a secure space, noting it as a place where "learning is simply good and enjoyable."[3][9] It maintains a tradition where some current parents were students, participates in programs like LHS Lebenshilfe since 2021, and upholds high facility standards with continuous IT improvements.[4][6][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free Grundschule in Berlin, admission follows local district assignment for children in the catchment area of Lichterfelde, Steglitz-Zehlendorf; contact the school directly for enrollment details via phone (030/93 95 19 40) or email (info@kastanie.schule.berlin.de).[1][2][3][8]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Grundschule Wilhelmsruh emphasizes creating healthy learning, working, and living conditions, with a strong focus on **health promotion** as a core profile, integrated into school life and culture through participation in the "Die gute gesunde Schule" program.[2][3][5][7] The school's pedagogical framework, adopted in 2009, addresses past challenges like inadequate facilities by prioritizing modern education, upbringing, and health promotion, fostering an open, life-oriented, and performance-driven environment with high communication, transparency, and appreciation among staff, students, and parents.[3][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Pankow's Wilhelmsruh district at Lessingstr. 44, 13158 Berlin, the school originated as a branch of Rudolf-Dörrier-Grundschule and was re-established as the 43rd Pankow school, addressing early issues with provisional buildings and limited playgrounds.[1][2][4][7] The main building, built in the early 1970s on a 15,000 m² site, was fully renovated from August 2015 to February 2018, with a 12-classroom extension opened in summer 2014; a new gym is under construction since 2025.[2][4] The playground, redesigned starting November 2008 with funding from federal programs and donations (e.g., Fielmann AG), features child-designed climbing structures, balance paths, sports fields, swings, sand/water play areas, and a nature garden to promote movement and health.[2][4] Facilities include specialized rooms for sciences, art, music, special education, library, computer lab, multi-purpose dining hall, movement room, rest room, and school garden; solar panels on roofs generate energy.[2][4][5] The community comprises **560 students**, **40 teachers**, and **20 educators**, mostly from education-oriented families, in a nearly three-track organization.[2][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Curriculum follows Berlin's primary school weekly schedule: early phase (grades 1-2) covers German, general studies, math, art, music, PE, and life skills; grade 3 adds English and swimming; grades 5-6 replace general studies with natural sciences and social sciences; grade 4 offers cycling exam.[6] Instruction uses a 40-minute block model (80-90 minutes) from periods 1-6, with 20 minutes daily for class team time addressing conflicts, projects, and celebrations via class councils.[3][6] Foreign languages include English (from grade 3) and French; health-focused profile with environmental education.[1][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
As an **open all-day school (Offene Ganztagsgrundschule)**, it provides before- and after-school care, including lunch, in cooperation with Pankower Früchtchen gGmbH at nearby HortEins Wilhelmsruh.[1][2][3] Early phase uses grade-specific and mixed-age learning groups; modern classrooms, computers, and specialized rooms support diverse methods for challenging, motivating lessons above Berlin averages.[3][5] Extracurriculars (AGs) include dance, handball, soccer, choir, theater, arts/comics, music, experimentation/wood workshops, student newspaper, historical searches, mediation training, school buddies, and learning workshops in languages/math/science.[5] Health emphasis includes movement promotion via playground and programs.[3][4][5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
School inspection reports highlight **outstanding school quality**, with results above Berlin averages in all criteria, clear structures, diverse methods, and successful task completion; strong school and lesson development, effective health profile integration.[3] Participation in "Die gute gesunde Schule" and vocational projects with ABB Ausbildungszentrum; modern facilities and solar energy contribute to sustainability.[2][3][7] Specific competition awards not detailed in content.[10]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free Grundschule** under Bezirksamt Pankow, admissions follow Berlin public school catchment areas (Einzugsbereiche); contact sekretariat@grundschule-wilhelmsruh.de or +49 30 91645320 for enrollment details via berlin.de resources.[1][5][8] Leadership: Holger Grenz.[1][5]
